52→export class CDPExtensionController {
53→  private readonly outPath: string
54→  private readonly browser: 'chrome' | 'edge' | 'chromium-based'
55→  private readonly cdpPort: number
56→  private readonly profilePath?: string
57→  private readonly extensionPaths?: string[]
58→  private readonly pipeIn?: Readable
59→  private readonly pipeOut?: Writable
60→  private cdp: CDPClient | null = null
61→  private extensionId: string | null = null
62→  private lastRuntimeReinjectionReport: Record<string, unknown> | null = null
63→  // Persistent content-script registration for future page targets (fresh
64→  // tabs, page reloads, cross-URL navigation). On each rebuild the reload
65→  // flow calls registerContentScriptsForFutureNavigations(rules). The
66→  // controller then keeps these rules in state and watches Target.* /
67→  // Runtime.executionContextCreated events so that whenever Chrome creates
68→  // the extension's isolated world on a matching URL we re-evaluate the
69→  // latest bundle source there. The wrapper's __EXTENSIONJS_DEV_REINJECT__
70→  // registry dedupes the static-cache-OLD + CDP-evaluated-NEW pair
71→  private activeContentScriptRules: ContentScriptTargetRule[] = []
72→  private contentScriptTargetListenerInstalled = false
73→  private watchedPageSessions = new Map<
74→    string,
75→    {targetId: string; url: string}
76→  >()
77→  // Per-session cache of execution contexts. `Runtime.enable` only re-emits
78→  // existing contexts on the first call per session — without caching, the
79→  // second call to resolveExecutionContextId() in a single rule iteration
80→  // returns no contexts and every rule after the first bails. The persistent
81→  // listener keeps the cache live across rule iterations and target lifetime.
82→  private sessionContexts = new Map<string, Map<number, unknown>>()
83→  private sessionContextsUnsubscribe = new Map<string, () => void>()
84→  // Sessions whose Runtime.enable just fired but whose initial executionContext
85→  // re-emission burst we want to ignore. After reloadMatchingTabsForContentScripts
86→  // already evaluated each rule into the target's existing contexts, we attach
87→  // a watch session and call Runtime.enable for navigation tracking — that
88→  // enable re-emits the same existing contexts as `executionContextCreated`,
89→  // and without suppression the persistent listener would re-evaluate every
90→  // rule a second time, racing with the just-created roots in MAIN world.
91→  private suppressInitialContextBurst = new Set<string>()
92→
93→  constructor(args: {
94→    outPath: string
95→    browser: 'chrome' | 'edge' | 'chromium-based'
96→    cdpPort: number
97→    profilePath?: string
98→    extensionPaths?: string[]
99→    pipeIn?: Readable
100→    pipeOut?: Writable
101→  }) {
102→    this.outPath = args.outPath
103→    this.browser = args.browser
104→    this.cdpPort = args.cdpPort
105→    this.profilePath = args.profilePath
106→    this.extensionPaths = args.extensionPaths
107→    this.pipeIn = args.pipeIn
108→    this.pipeOut = args.pipeOut
109→  }
110→
111→  async connect(): Promise<void> {
112→    if (this.cdp) return
113→    await this.connectFreshClient()
114→  }
115→
116→  async ensureLoaded(): Promise<ExtensionInfoResult> {
117→    if (!this.cdp) throw new Error('CDP not connected')
118→
119→    // Load unpacked extension from output path
120→    const exists = fs.existsSync(this.outPath)
121→    if (!exists) throw new Error(`Output path not found: ${this.outPath}`)
122→
123→    // CDP-first strategy: try Extensions.loadUnpacked (Chrome 126+ with
124→    // --enable-unsafe-extension-debugging), then fall back to target derivation
125→    // for older Chrome or when the CDP domain isn't available.
126→    if (!this.extensionId) {
127→      try {
128→        const {loadUnpackedIfNeeded} = await import('./ensure')
129→        const cdpId = await loadUnpackedIfNeeded(this.cdp, this.outPath)
130→        if (cdpId) this.extensionId = cdpId
131→      } catch {
132→        // Extensions.loadUnpacked not supported — fall through to target derivation
133→      }
134→    }
135→
136→    // Fallback: derive extensionId from Chrome targets (--load-extension path)
137→    if (!this.extensionId) {
138→      const id = await this.deriveExtensionIdFromTargets()
139→      if (id) this.extensionId = id
140→    }
141→
142→    if (this.extensionId) {
143→      const belongsToOutPath = this.extensionIdBelongsToOutPath(
144→        this.extensionId
145→      )
146→      if (belongsToOutPath === false) {
147→        this.extensionId = null
148→      }
149→    }
150→
151→    // If already known, verify info
152→    if (this.extensionId) {
153→      try {
154→        // Try Extensions domain first; if missing, fall back to manifest
155→        let info: {
156→          extensionInfo?: {name?: string; version?: string}
157→        } | null = null
158→
159→        try {
160→          info = await this.cdp.getExtensionInfo(this.extensionId)
161→        } catch {
162→          // ignore
163→        }
164→
165→        if (!info) {
166→          const manifest = JSON.parse(
167→            readTextFileCached(path.join(this.outPath, 'manifest.json'))
168→          )
169→
170→          return {
171→            extensionId: this.extensionId,
172→            name: manifest.name,
173→            version: manifest.version
174→          }
175→        }
176→
177→        return {
178→          extensionId: this.extensionId,
179→          name: info?.extensionInfo?.name,
180→          version: info?.extensionInfo?.version
181→        }
182→      } catch {
183→        // will reload
184→      }
185→    }
186→
187→    try {
188→      // If still unknown, derive from targets with a longer wait.
189→      if (!this.extensionId) {
190→        this.extensionId = await this.deriveExtensionIdFromTargets(20, 200)
191→      }
192→
193→      if (!this.extensionId) {
194→        throw new Error('Failed to determine extension ID via CDP')
195→      }
196→
197→      // After load/derivation, set up runtime/log domains
198→      await this.enableLogging()
199→
200→      // Prefer Extensions.getExtensionInfo; fallback to manifest
201→      let name: string | undefined
202→      let version: string | undefined
203→
204→      try {
205→        const info = await this.cdp.getExtensionInfo(this.extensionId)
206→        name = info?.extensionInfo?.name
207→        version = info?.extensionInfo?.version
208→      } catch (error) {
209→        try {
210→          const manifest = readManifestInfo(this.outPath)
211→          name = String(manifest?.name || '') || undefined
212→          version = String(manifest?.version || '') || undefined
213→        } catch (e2: unknown) {
214→          if (process.env.EXTENSION_AUTHOR_MODE === 'true') {
215→            console.warn(
216→              '[CDP] Fallback manifest read failed:',
217→              String((e2 as Error)?.message || e2)
218→            )
219→          }
220→        }
221→      }
222→
223→      return {extensionId: this.extensionId, name, version}
224→    } catch (error) {
225→      throw new Error(
226→        `Failed to load extension from ${path.resolve(this.outPath)}: ${String(
227→          (error as Error).message || error
228→        )}`
229→      )
230→    }
231→  }
232→
233→  private async deriveExtensionIdFromTargets(
234→    maxRetries = 20,
235→    backoffMs = 200
236→  ): Promise<string | null> {
237→    if (!this.cdp) return null
238→    return await deriveExtensionIdFromTargetsHelper(
239→      this.cdp,
240→      this.outPath,
241→      maxRetries,
242→      backoffMs,
243→      this.profilePath,
244→      this.extensionPaths
245→    )
246→  }
247→
248→  private normalizePath(input: string): string {
249→    try {
250→      return fs.realpathSync(path.resolve(input))
251→    } catch {
252→      return path.resolve(input)
253→    }
254→  }
255→
256→  private extensionIdBelongsToOutPath(extensionId: string): boolean | null {
257→    if (!this.profilePath || !extensionId) return null
258→
259→    const prefCandidates: string[] = []
260→    const addPrefCandidate = (dir: string) => {
261→      const prefPath = path.join(dir, 'Preferences')
262→      if (fs.existsSync(prefPath)) prefCandidates.push(prefPath)
263→    }
264→
265→    try {
266→      addPrefCandidate(this.profilePath)
267→      addPrefCandidate(path.join(this.profilePath, 'Default'))
268→      for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(this.profilePath)) {
269→        if (!/^Profile\s+\d+$/i.test(entry)) continue
270→        addPrefCandidate(path.join(this.profilePath, entry))
271→      }
272→    } catch {
273→      // Ignore profile listing errors.
274→    }
275→
276→    if (prefCandidates.length === 0) return null
277→
278→    const normalizedOutPath = this.normalizePath(this.outPath)
279→    for (const prefPath of prefCandidates) {
280→      try {
281→        const prefs = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(prefPath, 'utf-8'))
282→        const settings = prefs?.extensions?.settings
283→        const info = settings?.[extensionId]
284→        const storedPath = String(info?.path || '')
285→        if (!storedPath) continue
286→        return this.normalizePath(storedPath) === normalizedOutPath
287→      } catch {
288→        // Ignore malformed preference files.
289→      }
290→    }
291→
292→    return null
293→  }
294→
295→  getDeveloperModeStatus(): ChromiumDeveloperModeStatus {
296→    if (!this.profilePath) return 'unknown'
297→
298→    const prefCandidates: string[] = []
299→    const seen = new Set<string>()
300→
301→    const addPrefCandidate = (dir: string) => {
302→      const prefPath = path.join(dir, 'Preferences')
303→      if (!fs.existsSync(prefPath)) return
304→      const dedupeKey = path.resolve(prefPath)
305→      if (seen.has(dedupeKey)) return
306→      seen.add(dedupeKey)
307→      prefCandidates.push(prefPath)
308→    }
309→
310→    try {
311→      addPrefCandidate(this.profilePath)
312→      addPrefCandidate(path.join(this.profilePath, 'Default'))
313→
314→      for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(this.profilePath)) {
315→        if (!/^Profile\s+\d+$/i.test(entry)) continue
316→        addPrefCandidate(path.join(this.profilePath, entry))
317→      }
318→    } catch {
319→      // Ignore profile listing errors.
320→    }
321→
322→    for (const prefPath of prefCandidates) {
323→      try {
324→        const prefs = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(prefPath, 'utf-8'))
325→        const uiFlag = prefs?.extensions?.ui?.developer_mode
326→
327→        if (typeof uiFlag === 'boolean') {
328→          return uiFlag ? 'enabled' : 'disabled'
329→        }
330→
331→        const legacyFlag = prefs?.extensions?.developer_mode
332→        if (typeof legacyFlag === 'boolean') {
333→          return legacyFlag ? 'enabled' : 'disabled'
334→        }
335→      } catch {
336→        // Ignore malformed preference files.
337→      }
338→    }
339→
340→    return 'unknown'
341→  }
342→
343→  getLastRuntimeReinjectionReport(): Record<string, unknown> | null {
344→    return this.lastRuntimeReinjectionReport
345→  }
346→
347→  async reloadMatchingTabsForContentScripts(
348→    rules: ContentScriptTargetRule[],
349→    options: {
350→      preferPageReload?: boolean
351→      allowCoarseCleanup?: boolean
352→      sourceOverridesByBundleId?: Record<string, string>
353→    } = {}
354→  ): Promise<number> {
355→    if (!this.cdp || rules.length === 0) return 0
356→
357→    let reinjectedTabs = 0
358→    const targets = await this.cdp.getTargets()
359→
360→    for (const target of targets || []) {
361→      const targetType = String((target as any)?.type || '')
362→      const targetId = String((target as any)?.targetId || '')
363→      const targetUrl = String((target as any)?.url || '')
364→
365→      if (targetType !== 'page' || !targetId || !targetUrl) continue
366→      const matchingRules = rules.filter((rule) =>
367→        urlMatchesAnyContentScriptRule(targetUrl, [rule])
368→      )
369→
370→      if (matchingRules.length === 0) continue
371→
372→      try {
373→        const sessionId = await this.cdp.attachToTarget(targetId)
374→        let reinjected = false
375→
376→        if (options.preferPageReload) {
377→          reinjected = await this.reloadPageTarget(sessionId)
378→        } else {
379→          for (const [index, rule] of matchingRules.entries()) {
380→            const didReinject = await this.reinjectContentScriptRule(
381→              sessionId,
382→              rule,
383→              options.allowCoarseCleanup ?? index === 0,
384→              options.sourceOverridesByBundleId
385→            )
386→            reinjected = reinjected || didReinject
387→          }
388→
389→          if (!reinjected) {
390→            reinjected = await this.reloadPageTarget(sessionId)
391→          }
392→        }
393→
394→        if (reinjected) {
395→          reinjectedTabs += 1
396→        }
397→      } catch (error) {
398→        if (process.env.EXTENSION_AUTHOR_MODE === 'true') {
399→          console.warn(
400→            `[CDP] Failed to reinject content scripts into ${targetUrl}: ${String(
401→              (error as Error)?.message || error
402→            )}`
403→          )
404→        }
405→      }
406→    }
407→
408→    return reinjectedTabs
409→  }
410→
411→  /**
412→   * Install a persistent CDP listener that re-injects the latest content-
413→   * script bundle into any page that navigates to a URL matching a rule —
414→   * including fresh tabs, full page reloads, and same-tab URL changes.
415→   *
416→   * Chrome binds its static `content_scripts` list to the bytes it read when
417→   * the extension was loaded, and those cached bytes don't change when
418→   * Rspack rewrites the hashed bundle on edit. We can't reload the extension
419→   * without losing SW state and closing popup/sidebar. So for every matching
420→   * navigation we wait for Chrome to create the extension's isolated world
421→   * (which happens at `document_start` just before the cached content script
422→   * runs) and evaluate the fresh bundle source in that exact world. The
423→   * wrapper's `__EXTENSIONJS_DEV_REINJECT__` registry keyed on the canonical
424→   * bundle id unmounts the stale generation before mounting the new one, so
425→   * what the user sees is the latest build.
426→   *
427→   * Called on every `content-scripts` reload instruction with the latest
428→   * rules. Safe to call repeatedly — rules are simply replaced.
429→   */
430→  async registerContentScriptsForFutureNavigations(
431→    rules: ContentScriptTargetRule[]
432→  ): Promise<void> {
433→    this.activeContentScriptRules = [...rules]
434→    if (!this.cdp) return
435→
436→    if (!this.contentScriptTargetListenerInstalled) {
437→      this.contentScriptTargetListenerInstalled = true
438→      this.installContentScriptTargetListener()
439→    }
440→
441→    // Proactively attach to page targets that already match so their next
442→    // navigation (or an in-flight one) is covered without waiting for a
443→    // `targetInfoChanged` event.
444→    try {
445→      const targets = await this.cdp.getTargets()
446→      for (const target of targets || []) {
447→        if (String((target as any)?.type || '') !== 'page') continue
448→        const targetId = String((target as any)?.targetId || '')
449→        const url = String((target as any)?.url || '')
450→        if (!targetId || !url) continue
451→        if (!this.urlMatchesAnyActiveRule(url)) continue
452→        await this.ensureWatchedPageSession(targetId, url)
453→      }
454→    } catch {
455→      // Best-effort pre-attach; the listener handles future targets.
456→    }
457→  }
458→
459→  private installContentScriptTargetListener(): void {
460→    if (!this.cdp) return
461→    this.cdp.onProtocolEvent((raw: Record<string, unknown>) => {
462→      const method = String((raw as {method?: string}).method || '')
463→      const params = (raw as {params?: any}).params
464→      const sessionId = String((raw as {sessionId?: string}).sessionId || '')
465→
466→      if (
467→        method === 'Target.targetCreated' ||
468→        method === 'Target.targetInfoChanged'
469→      ) {
470→        const targetInfo = params?.targetInfo
471→        if (!targetInfo || targetInfo.type !== 'page') return
472→        const targetId = String(targetInfo.targetId || '')
473→        const url = String(targetInfo.url || '')
474→        if (!targetId) return
475→        if (!this.urlMatchesAnyActiveRule(url)) {
476→          // Update URL for tracked session so a future context created on a
477→          // now-non-matching URL is skipped.
478→          for (const info of this.watchedPageSessions.values()) {
479→            if (info.targetId === targetId) info.url = url
480→          }
481→          return
482→        }
483→        this.ensureWatchedPageSession(targetId, url).catch(() => {
484→          // Target may have closed mid-flight; ignore.
485→        })
486→        return
487→      }
488→
489→      if (method === 'Runtime.executionContextCreated') {
490→        if (!sessionId) return
491→        const session = this.watchedPageSessions.get(sessionId)
492→        if (!session) return
493→        // Initial Runtime.enable on a freshly-attached session re-emits every
494→        // existing context as if it were just created. The reinject loop has
495→        // already evaluated each rule into those contexts, so re-evaluating
496→        // here would race with the just-mounted roots — observable as MAIN
497→        // world content disappearing milliseconds after a file edit.
498→        if (this.suppressInitialContextBurst.has(sessionId)) return
499→        const context = params?.context
500→        if (!context) return
501→        this.evaluateContentScriptOnNewContext(
502→          sessionId,
503→          session,
504→          context
505→        ).catch(() => {
506→          // Best-effort; next navigation will re-try.
507→        })
508→        return
509→      }
510→
511→      if (method === 'Target.detachedFromTarget') {
512→        if (sessionId) {
513→          this.watchedPageSessions.delete(sessionId)
514→          this.releaseSessionContexts(sessionId)
515→          this.suppressInitialContextBurst.delete(sessionId)
516→        }
517→      }
518→    })
519→  }
520→
521→  private urlMatchesAnyActiveRule(url: string): boolean {
522→    if (!url || this.activeContentScriptRules.length === 0) return false
523→    return this.activeContentScriptRules.some((rule) =>
524→      urlMatchesAnyContentScriptRule(url, [rule])
525→    )
526→  }
527→
528→  private async ensureWatchedPageSession(
529→    targetId: string,
530→    url: string
531→  ): Promise<void> {
532→    if (!this.cdp) return
533→    // Already attached to this target? Update URL and bail.
534→    for (const info of this.watchedPageSessions.values()) {
535→      if (info.targetId === targetId) {
536→        info.url = url
537→        return
538→      }
539→    }
540→
541→    let sessionId: string
542→    try {
543→      sessionId = await this.cdp.attachToTarget(targetId)
544→    } catch {
545→      return
546→    }
547→    this.watchedPageSessions.set(sessionId, {targetId, url})
548→    // Suppress the executionContextCreated burst that Runtime.enable fires for
549→    // already-existing contexts — those have already been handled by the
550→    // reinject loop. Anything emitted >250ms after enable is a genuine new
551→    // context (page navigation, iframe creation) and gets the normal eval.
552→    this.suppressInitialContextBurst.add(sessionId)
553→    try {
554→      await this.cdp.sendCommand('Runtime.enable', {}, sessionId)
555→      setTimeout(() => {
556→        this.suppressInitialContextBurst.delete(sessionId)
557→      }, 250)
558→    } catch {
559→      // If Runtime.enable fails the session is likely stale; clean up.
560→      this.watchedPageSessions.delete(sessionId)
561→      this.suppressInitialContextBurst.delete(sessionId)
562→    }
563→  }
564→
565→  private async evaluateContentScriptOnNewContext(
566→    sessionId: string,
567→    session: {targetId: string; url: string},
568→    context: {
569→      id?: number
570→      origin?: string
571→      auxData?: {type?: string; isDefault?: boolean; frameId?: string}
572→    }
573→  ): Promise<void> {
574→    if (!this.cdp) return
575→    const contextId = context.id
576→    if (typeof contextId !== 'number') return
577→    if (!this.urlMatchesAnyActiveRule(session.url)) return
578→
579→    const auxData = context.auxData || {}
580→    const origin = String(context.origin || '')
581→    const extensionId =
582→      this.extensionId || (await this.deriveExtensionIdFromTargets())
583→    const extensionOrigin = extensionId
584→      ? `chrome-extension://${extensionId}`
585→      : ''
586→
587→    // Find rules that match this URL AND target the world of this context.
588→    const matchingRules = this.activeContentScriptRules.filter((rule) => {
589→      if (!urlMatchesAnyContentScriptRule(session.url, [rule])) return false
590→      if (rule.world === 'main') {
591→        return auxData.type === 'default' && auxData.isDefault === true
592→      }
593→      return auxData.type === 'isolated' && origin === extensionOrigin
594→    })
595→
596→    if (matchingRules.length === 0) return
597→
598→    for (const rule of matchingRules) {
599→      const bundlePath = resolveEmittedContentScriptFile(
600→        this.outPath,
601→        rule.index,
602→        'js'
603→      )
604→      if (!bundlePath || !fs.existsSync(bundlePath)) continue
605→      const source = this.patchReinjectSourceForInvalidatedRuntime(
606→        readTextFileCached(bundlePath)
607→      )
608→      if (!source.trim()) continue
609→      const bundleId = getCanonicalContentScriptEntryName(rule.index)
610→      // allowCoarseCleanup=true: fresh navigation, nothing to preserve.
611→      const expression = this.buildReinjectExpression(bundleId, source, true)
612→      try {
613→        await this.cdp.evaluateInContext(sessionId, expression, contextId)
614→      } catch {
615→        // Best-effort; ignore per-rule failures.
616→      }
617→    }
618→  }
619→
620→  async reinjectMatchingTabsViaExtensionRuntime(
621→    rules: ContentScriptTargetRule[]
622→  ): Promise<number> {
623→    if (!this.cdp || rules.length === 0) {
624→      this.lastRuntimeReinjectionReport = {
625→        phase: 'skipped',
626→        reason: 'missing_cdp_or_rules',
627→        hasCdp: !!this.cdp,
628→        ruleCount: rules.length
629→      }
630→      return 0
631→    }
632→
633→    const targets = await this.cdp.getTargets()
634→    const matchingUrlsByRule = new Map<number, Set<string>>()
635→
636→    for (const target of targets || []) {
637→      const targetType = String((target as any)?.type || '')
638→      const targetUrl = String((target as any)?.url || '')
639→      if (targetType !== 'page' || !targetUrl) continue
640→
641→      for (const rule of rules) {
642→        if (!urlMatchesAnyContentScriptRule(targetUrl, [rule])) continue
643→        const urls = matchingUrlsByRule.get(rule.index) || new Set<string>()
644→        urls.add(targetUrl)
645→        matchingUrlsByRule.set(rule.index, urls)
646→      }
647→    }
648→
649→    const payload = rules
650→      .map((rule) => {
651→        const jsPath = resolveEmittedContentScriptFile(
652→          this.outPath,
653→          rule.index,
654→          'js'
655→        )
656→        const cssPath = resolveEmittedContentScriptFile(
657→          this.outPath,
658→          rule.index,
659→          'css'
660→        )
661→        if (!jsPath || !fs.existsSync(jsPath)) return null
662→        const jsFile = path.relative(this.outPath, jsPath).replace(/\\/g, '/')
663→        const cssFile =
664→          cssPath && fs.existsSync(cssPath)
665→            ? path.relative(this.outPath, cssPath).replace(/\\/g, '/')
666→            : null
667→        const jsSource = readTextFileCached(jsPath)
668→        const buildTokenMatch = jsSource.match(
669→          /__EXTENSIONJS_REINJECT_BUILD_TOKEN\s*=\s*"([^"]+)"/
670→        )
671→        const proofMatch =
672→          jsSource.match(
673→            /extjs-chromium-live-content-css-modules:script-primary-\d+/
674→          ) || jsSource.match(/Live Update Proof [A-Za-z0-9_-]+/)
675→
676→        return {
677→          index: rule.index,
678→          world: rule.world,
679→          matches: [...rule.matches],
680→          urls: Array.from(matchingUrlsByRule.get(rule.index) || []).sort(),
681→          jsFile,
682→          cssFile,
683→          buildToken: buildTokenMatch?.[1] || null,
684→          proofMarker: proofMatch?.[0] || null
685→        }
686→      })
687→      .filter(
688→        (
689→          entry
690→        ): entry is {
691→          index: number
692→          world: 'extension' | 'main'
693→          matches: string[]
694→          urls: string[]
695→          jsFile: string
696→          cssFile: string | null
697→          buildToken: string | null
698→          proofMarker: string | null
699→        } => !!entry && entry.urls.length > 0
700→      )
701→
702→    if (payload.length === 0) {
703→      this.lastRuntimeReinjectionReport = {
704→        phase: 'skipped',
705→        reason: 'no_payload',
706→        ruleCount: rules.length
707→      }
708→      if (process.env.EXTENSION_AUTHOR_MODE === 'true') {
709→        console.log('[CDP] extension-runtime reinjection skipped: no payload')
710→      }
711→      return 0
712→    }
713→
714→    const runtimeTarget = await this.attachToExtensionRuntimeTarget()
715→    if (!runtimeTarget) {
716→      const targetSummary = (targets || [])
717→        .map((target) => ({
718→          type: String((target as any)?.type || ''),
719→          url: String((target as any)?.url || '')
720→        }))
721→        .filter((entry) => entry.type || entry.url)
722→      this.lastRuntimeReinjectionReport = {
723→        phase: 'unavailable',
724→        reason: 'no_runtime_target',
725→        extensionId: this.extensionId,
726→        targets: targetSummary
727→      }
728→      if (process.env.EXTENSION_AUTHOR_MODE === 'true') {
729→        console.log(
730→          `[CDP] extension-runtime reinjection unavailable: ${JSON.stringify({
731→            extensionId: this.extensionId,
732→            targets: targetSummary
733→          })}`
734→        )
735→      }
736→      return 0
737→    }
738→
739→    const result = await this.cdp.evaluate(
740→      runtimeTarget.sessionId,
741→      `(() => (async () => {
742→        const payload = ${JSON.stringify(payload)};
743→        const chromeRuntime =
744→          typeof globalThis === 'object' && globalThis && globalThis.chrome
745→            ? globalThis.chrome
746→            : null;
747→        const runtimeChrome =
748→          chromeRuntime &&
749→          chromeRuntime.scripting &&
750→          chromeRuntime.tabs
751→            ? chromeRuntime
752→            : null;
753→        if (!runtimeChrome) {
754→          return {
755→            reinjectedTabs: 0,
756→            hasRuntime: false,
757→            hasChrome: !!chromeRuntime,
758→            hasScripting: !!(chromeRuntime && chromeRuntime.scripting),
759→            hasTabs: !!(chromeRuntime && chromeRuntime.tabs),
760→            entries: payload.length,
761→            matches: []
762→          };
763→        }
764→        let reinjectedTabs = 0;
765→        const matches = [];
766→        for (const entry of payload) {
767→          const queriedTabs = await runtimeChrome.tabs.query(
768→            entry.matches.length > 0 ? { url: entry.matches } : {}
769→          );
770→          const matchingTabs = queriedTabs.filter((tab) => {
771→            if (!tab || typeof tab.id !== 'number') return false;
772→            if (typeof tab.url !== 'string' || !tab.url) return true;
773→            return entry.urls.length === 0 || entry.urls.includes(tab.url);
774→          });
775→          matches.push({
776→            index: entry.index,
777→            queriedTabs: queriedTabs.length,
778→            matchingTabs: matchingTabs.length
779→          });
780→          for (const tab of matchingTabs) {
781→            const target = { tabId: tab.id, allFrames: false };
782→            if (entry.cssFile) {
783→              try {
784→                await runtimeChrome.scripting.insertCSS({
785→                  target,
786→                  files: [entry.cssFile]
787→                });
788→              } catch (error) {}
789→            }
790→            await runtimeChrome.scripting.executeScript({
791→              target,
792→              files: [entry.jsFile],
793→              world: entry.world === 'main' ? 'MAIN' : 'ISOLATED'
794→            });
795→            reinjectedTabs += 1;
796→          }
797→        }
798→        return {
799→          reinjectedTabs,
800→          hasRuntime: true,
801→          entries: payload.length,
802→          matches
803→        };
804→      })())()`,
805→      {awaitPromise: true}
806→    )
807→
808→    this.lastRuntimeReinjectionReport = {
809→      phase: 'evaluated',
810→      targetType: runtimeTarget.targetType,
811→      targetUrl: runtimeTarget.targetUrl,
812→      result:
813→        result && typeof result === 'object'
814→          ? JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(result))
815→          : result
816→    }
817→
818→    if (process.env.EXTENSION_AUTHOR_MODE === 'true') {
819→      console.log(
820→        `[CDP] extension-runtime reinjection result: ${JSON.stringify({
821→          targetType: runtimeTarget.targetType,
822→          targetUrl: runtimeTarget.targetUrl,
823→          result
824→        })}`
825→      )
826→    }
827→
828→    if (typeof result === 'number') return result
829→    if (result && typeof result === 'object') {
830→      const reinjectedTabs = Number((result as any).reinjectedTabs)
831→      return Number.isFinite(reinjectedTabs) ? reinjectedTabs : 0
832→    }
833→    return 0
834→  }
835→
836→  private async reloadPageTarget(sessionId: string): Promise<boolean> {
837→    if (!this.cdp) return false
838→
839→    try {
840→      await this.cdp.sendCommand(
841→        'Page.reload',
842→        {
843→          ignoreCache: true
844→        },
845→        sessionId
846→      )
847→      return true
848→    } catch {
849→      return false
850→    }
851→  }
852→
853→  /**
854→   * Replay previously-tracked `chrome.scripting.executeScript` calls from the
855→   * background SW. The dev-only `__extjsScriptsReplay` shim (injected by
856→   * `feature-scripts/InjectScriptsReplayShim`) keeps an in-memory registry of
857→   * `executeScript({target.tabId, files, world})` calls the user's SW has
858→   * made. When the user edits a file in `/scripts/*` we evaluate that
859→   * registry inside the SW and re-issue every matching injection — bringing
860→   * programmatic injection HMR closer to parity with declarative content
861→   * scripts.
862→   *
863→   * No-op if no scripts changed, if the shim isn't installed (e.g. the SW
864→   * was injected before the shim shipped, or the user disabled reload), or
865→   * if no runtime target is reachable.
866→   */
867→  async replayProgrammaticScripts(
868→    changedScriptFiles: string[]
869→  ): Promise<{replayed: number}> {
870→    if (!this.cdp) return {replayed: 0}
871→    if (!Array.isArray(changedScriptFiles) || changedScriptFiles.length === 0) {
872→      return {replayed: 0}
873→    }
874→    const runtimeTarget = await this.attachToExtensionRuntimeTarget()
875→    if (!runtimeTarget) return {replayed: 0}
876→
877→    const result = await this.cdp.evaluate(
878→      runtimeTarget.sessionId,
879→      `(() => (async () => {
880→        if (typeof globalThis !== "object" || !globalThis) return {ok: false, reason: "no globalThis"};
881→        if (typeof globalThis.__extjsScriptsReplay !== "function") {
882→          return {ok: false, reason: "shim_missing"};
883→        }
884→        try {
885→          const outcome = await globalThis.__extjsScriptsReplay(${JSON.stringify(
886→            changedScriptFiles
887→          )});
888→          const list = Array.isArray(outcome) ? outcome : [];
889→          return {
890→            ok: true,
891→            replayed: list.filter((entry) => entry && entry.ok).length,
892→            failures: list.filter((entry) => entry && entry.ok === false).length
893→          };
894→        } catch (error) {
895→          return {ok: false, reason: "eval_error", message: String((error && error.message) || error)};
896→        }
897→      })())()`,
898→      {awaitPromise: true}
899→    )
900→    if (process.env.EXTENSION_AUTHOR_MODE === 'true') {
901→      try {
902→        console.log(
903→          `[CDP] scripts replay result: ${JSON.stringify({
904→            changedScriptFiles,
905→            result
906→          })}`
907→        )
908→      } catch {
909→        // ignore
910→      }
911→    }
912→    if (result && typeof result === 'object') {
913→      const replayed = Number((result as any).replayed)
914→      return {replayed: Number.isFinite(replayed) ? replayed : 0}
915→    }
916→    return {replayed: 0}
917→  }
918→
919→  async hardReload(): Promise<boolean> {
920→    if (!this.extensionId) return false
921→
922→    try {
923→      if (!this.cdp) {
924→        await this.connectFreshClient()
925→      }
926→
927→      if (this.cdp && (await this.cdp.forceReloadExtension(this.extensionId))) {
928→        return true
929→      }
930→    } catch {
931→      // Retry once with a fresh CDP session below.
932→    }
933→
934→    try {
935→      await this.reconnectForReload()
936→      return Boolean(
937→        this.cdp &&
938→          this.extensionId &&
939→          (await this.cdp.forceReloadExtension(this.extensionId))
940→      )
941→    } catch {
942→      return false
943→    }
944→  }
945→
946→  private async attachToExtensionRuntimeTarget(): Promise<
947→    | {
948→        sessionId: string
949→        targetType: string
950→        targetUrl: string
951→      }
952→    | undefined
953→  > {
954→    if (!this.cdp) return undefined
955→    if (!this.extensionId) {
956→      this.extensionId = await this.deriveExtensionIdFromTargets(10, 150)
957→    }
958→    if (!this.extensionId) return undefined
959→
960→    const extensionOrigin = `chrome-extension://${this.extensionId}/`
961→
962→    for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 20; attempt++) {
963→      const targets = await this.cdp.getTargets()
964→      const runtimeTargets = (targets || []).filter((target) => {
965→        const targetType = String((target as any)?.type || '')
966→        const targetId = String((target as any)?.targetId || '')
967→        return (
968→          (targetType === 'service_worker' ||
969→            targetType === 'background_page' ||
970→            targetType === 'worker') &&
971→          !!targetId
972→        )
973→      })
974→      const runtimeTarget =
975→        runtimeTargets.find((target) =>
976→          String((target as any)?.url || '').startsWith(extensionOrigin)
977→        ) ||
978→        runtimeTargets.find((target) =>
979→          String((target as any)?.url || '').startsWith('chrome-extension://')
980→        )
981→
982→      if (runtimeTarget) {
983→        try {
984→          const sessionId = await this.cdp.attachToTarget(
985→            String((runtimeTarget as any).targetId)
986→          )
987→          await this.cdp.sendCommand('Runtime.enable', {}, sessionId)
988→          return {
989→            sessionId,
990→            targetType: String((runtimeTarget as any)?.type || ''),
991→            targetUrl: String((runtimeTarget as any)?.url || '')
992→          }
993→        } catch {
994→          // Retry on the next pass.
995→        }
996→      }
997→
998→      this.extensionId =
999→        (await this.deriveExtensionIdFromTargets(4, 100)) || this.extensionId
1000→      await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 250))

9→import * as path from 'path'
10→import * as fs from 'fs'
11→import type {Readable, Writable} from 'stream'
12→import * as messages from '../../../browsers-lib/messages'

14→  type ContentScriptTargetRule,
15→  resolveEmittedContentScriptFile,
16→  urlMatchesAnyContentScriptRule
17→} from '../../../browsers-lib/content-script-targets'

26→  extensionId: string
27→  name?: string
28→  version?: string

31→export type ChromiumDeveloperModeStatus = 'enabled' | 'disabled' | 'unknown'

33→const __fileReadCache = new Map<string, {key: string; text: string}>()
34→
35→function readTextFileCached(filePath: string): string {
36→  try {
37→    const stat = fs.statSync(filePath)
38→    const key = `${stat.mtimeMs}:${stat.size}`
39→    const cached = __fileReadCache.get(filePath)
40→
41→    if (cached && cached.key === key) return cached.text
42→
43→    const text = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')
44→    __fileReadCache.set(filePath, {key, text})
45→
46→    return text
47→  } catch {
48→    return fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')
49→  }
50→}

52→export class CDPExtensionController {
53→  private readonly outPath: string
54→  private readonly browser: 'chrome' | 'edge' | 'chromium-based'
55→  private readonly cdpPort: number
56→  private readonly profilePath?: string
57→  private readonly extensionPaths?: string[]
58→  private readonly pipeIn?: Readable
59→  private readonly pipeOut?: Writable
60→  private cdp: CDPClient | null = null
61→  private extensionId: string | null = null
62→  private lastRuntimeReinjectionReport: Record<string, unknown> | null = null
63→  // Persistent content-script registration for future page targets (fresh
64→  // tabs, page reloads, cross-URL navigation). On each rebuild the reload
65→  // flow calls registerContentScriptsForFutureNavigations(rules). The
66→  // controller then keeps these rules in state and watches Target.* /
67→  // Runtime.executionContextCreated events so that whenever Chrome creates
68→  // the extension's isolated world on a matching URL we re-evaluate the
69→  // latest bundle source there. The wrapper's __EXTENSIONJS_DEV_REINJECT__
70→  // registry dedupes the static-cache-OLD + CDP-evaluated-NEW pair
71→  private activeContentScriptRules: ContentScriptTargetRule[] = []
72→  private contentScriptTargetListenerInstalled = false
73→  private watchedPageSessions = new Map<
74→    string,
75→    {targetId: string; url: string}
76→  >()
77→  // Per-session cache of execution contexts. `Runtime.enable` only re-emits
78→  // existing contexts on the first call per session — without caching, the
79→  // second call to resolveExecutionContextId() in a single rule iteration
80→  // returns no contexts and every rule after the first bails. The persistent
81→  // listener keeps the cache live across rule iterations and target lifetime.
82→  private sessionContexts = new Map<string, Map<number, unknown>>()
83→  private sessionContextsUnsubscribe = new Map<string, () => void>()
84→  // Sessions whose Runtime.enable just fired but whose initial executionContext
85→  // re-emission burst we want to ignore. After reloadMatchingTabsForContentScripts
86→  // already evaluated each rule into the target's existing contexts, we attach
87→  // a watch session and call Runtime.enable for navigation tracking — that
88→  // enable re-emits the same existing contexts as `executionContextCreated`,
89→  // and without suppression the persistent listener would re-evaluate every
90→  // rule a second time, racing with the just-created roots in MAIN world.
91→  private suppressInitialContextBurst = new Set<string>()
92→
93→  constructor(args: {
94→    outPath: string
95→    browser: 'chrome' | 'edge' | 'chromium-based'
96→    cdpPort: number
97→    profilePath?: string
98→    extensionPaths?: string[]
99→    pipeIn?: Readable
100→    pipeOut?: Writable
101→  }) {
102→    this.outPath = args.outPath
103→    this.browser = args.browser
104→    this.cdpPort = args.cdpPort
105→    this.profilePath = args.profilePath
106→    this.extensionPaths = args.extensionPaths
107→    this.pipeIn = args.pipeIn
108→    this.pipeOut = args.pipeOut
109→  }
110→
111→  async connect(): Promise<void> {
112→    if (this.cdp) return
113→    await this.connectFreshClient()
114→  }
115→
116→  async ensureLoaded(): Promise<ExtensionInfoResult> {
117→    if (!this.cdp) throw new Error('CDP not connected')
118→
119→    // Load unpacked extension from output path
120→    const exists = fs.existsSync(this.outPath)
121→    if (!exists) throw new Error(`Output path not found: ${this.outPath}`)
122→
123→    // CDP-first strategy: try Extensions.loadUnpacked (Chrome 126+ with
124→    // --enable-unsafe-extension-debugging), then fall back to target derivation
125→    // for older Chrome or when the CDP domain isn't available.
126→    if (!this.extensionId) {
127→      try {
128→        const {loadUnpackedIfNeeded} = await import('./ensure')
129→        const cdpId = await loadUnpackedIfNeeded(this.cdp, this.outPath)
130→        if (cdpId) this.extensionId = cdpId
131→      } catch {
132→        // Extensions.loadUnpacked not supported — fall through to target derivation
133→      }
134→    }
135→
136→    // Fallback: derive extensionId from Chrome targets (--load-extension path)
137→    if (!this.extensionId) {
138→      const id = await this.deriveExtensionIdFromTargets()
139→      if (id) this.extensionId = id
140→    }
141→
142→    if (this.extensionId) {
143→      const belongsToOutPath = this.extensionIdBelongsToOutPath(
144→        this.extensionId
145→      )
146→      if (belongsToOutPath === false) {
147→        this.extensionId = null
148→      }
149→    }
150→
151→    // If already known, verify info
152→    if (this.extensionId) {
153→      try {
154→        // Try Extensions domain first; if missing, fall back to manifest
155→        let info: {
156→          extensionInfo?: {name?: string; version?: string}
157→        } | null = null
158→
159→        try {
160→          info = await this.cdp.getExtensionInfo(this.extensionId)
161→        } catch {
162→          // ignore
163→        }
164→
165→        if (!info) {
166→          const manifest = JSON.parse(
167→            readTextFileCached(path.join(this.outPath, 'manifest.json'))
168→          )
169→
170→          return {
171→            extensionId: this.extensionId,
172→            name: manifest.name,
173→            version: manifest.version
174→          }
175→        }
176→
177→        return {
178→          extensionId: this.extensionId,
179→          name: info?.extensionInfo?.name,
180→          version: info?.extensionInfo?.version
181→        }
182→      } catch {
183→        // will reload
184→      }
185→    }
186→
187→    try {
188→      // If still unknown, derive from targets with a longer wait.
189→      if (!this.extensionId) {
190→        this.extensionId = await this.deriveExtensionIdFromTargets(20, 200)
191→      }
192→
193→      if (!this.extensionId) {
194→        throw new Error('Failed to determine extension ID via CDP')
195→      }
196→
197→      // After load/derivation, set up runtime/log domains
198→      await this.enableLogging()
199→
200→      // Prefer Extensions.getExtensionInfo; fallback to manifest
201→      let name: string | undefined
202→      let version: string | undefined
203→
204→      try {
205→        const info = await this.cdp.getExtensionInfo(this.extensionId)
206→        name = info?.extensionInfo?.name
207→        version = info?.extensionInfo?.version
208→      } catch (error) {
209→        try {
210→          const manifest = readManifestInfo(this.outPath)
211→          name = String(manifest?.name || '') || undefined
212→          version = String(manifest?.version || '') || undefined
213→        } catch (e2: unknown) {
214→          if (process.env.EXTENSION_AUTHOR_MODE === 'true') {
215→            console.warn(
216→              '[CDP] Fallback manifest read failed:',
217→              String((e2 as Error)?.message || e2)
218→            )
219→          }
220→        }
221→      }
222→
223→      return {extensionId: this.extensionId, name, version}
224→    } catch (error) {
225→      throw new Error(
226→        `Failed to load extension from ${path.resolve(this.outPath)}: ${String(
227→          (error as Error).message || error
228→        )}`
229→      )
230→    }
231→  }
232→
233→  private async deriveExtensionIdFromTargets(
234→    maxRetries = 20,
235→    backoffMs = 200
236→  ): Promise<string | null> {
237→    if (!this.cdp) return null
238→    return await deriveExtensionIdFromTargetsHelper(
239→      this.cdp,
240→      this.outPath,
241→      maxRetries,
242→      backoffMs,
243→      this.profilePath,
244→      this.extensionPaths
245→    )
246→  }
247→
248→  private normalizePath(input: string): string {
249→    try {
250→      return fs.realpathSync(path.resolve(input))
251→    } catch {
252→      return path.resolve(input)
253→    }
254→  }
255→
256→  private extensionIdBelongsToOutPath(extensionId: string): boolean | null {
257→    if (!this.profilePath || !extensionId) return null
258→
259→    const prefCandidates: string[] = []
260→    const addPrefCandidate = (dir: string) => {
261→      const prefPath = path.join(dir, 'Preferences')
262→      if (fs.existsSync(prefPath)) prefCandidates.push(prefPath)
263→    }
264→
265→    try {
266→      addPrefCandidate(this.profilePath)
267→      addPrefCandidate(path.join(this.profilePath, 'Default'))
268→      for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(this.profilePath)) {
269→        if (!/^Profile\s+\d+$/i.test(entry)) continue
270→        addPrefCandidate(path.join(this.profilePath, entry))
271→      }
272→    } catch {
273→      // Ignore profile listing errors.
274→    }
275→
276→    if (prefCandidates.length === 0) return null
277→
278→    const normalizedOutPath = this.normalizePath(this.outPath)
279→    for (const prefPath of prefCandidates) {
280→      try {
281→        const prefs = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(prefPath, 'utf-8'))
282→        const settings = prefs?.extensions?.settings
283→        const info = settings?.[extensionId]
284→        const storedPath = String(info?.path || '')
285→        if (!storedPath) continue
286→        return this.normalizePath(storedPath) === normalizedOutPath
287→      } catch {
288→        // Ignore malformed preference files.
289→      }
290→    }
291→
292→    return null
293→  }
294→
295→  getDeveloperModeStatus(): ChromiumDeveloperModeStatus {
296→    if (!this.profilePath) return 'unknown'
297→
298→    const prefCandidates: string[] = []
299→    const seen = new Set<string>()
300→
301→    const addPrefCandidate = (dir: string) => {
302→      const prefPath = path.join(dir, 'Preferences')
303→      if (!fs.existsSync(prefPath)) return
304→      const dedupeKey = path.resolve(prefPath)
305→      if (seen.has(dedupeKey)) return
306→      seen.add(dedupeKey)
307→      prefCandidates.push(prefPath)
308→    }
309→
310→    try {
311→      addPrefCandidate(this.profilePath)
312→      addPrefCandidate(path.join(this.profilePath, 'Default'))
313→
314→      for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(this.profilePath)) {
315→        if (!/^Profile\s+\d+$/i.test(entry)) continue
316→        addPrefCandidate(path.join(this.profilePath, entry))
317→      }
318→    } catch {
319→      // Ignore profile listing errors.
320→    }
321→
322→    for (const prefPath of prefCandidates) {
323→      try {
324→        const prefs = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(prefPath, 'utf-8'))
325→        const uiFlag = prefs?.extensions?.ui?.developer_mode
326→
327→        if (typeof uiFlag === 'boolean') {
328→          return uiFlag ? 'enabled' : 'disabled'
329→        }
330→
331→        const legacyFlag = prefs?.extensions?.developer_mode
332→        if (typeof legacyFlag === 'boolean') {
333→          return legacyFlag ? 'enabled' : 'disabled'
334→        }
335→      } catch {
336→        // Ignore malformed preference files.
337→      }
338→    }
339→
340→    return 'unknown'
341→  }
342→
343→  getLastRuntimeReinjectionReport(): Record<string, unknown> | null {
344→    return this.lastRuntimeReinjectionReport
345→  }
346→
347→  async reloadMatchingTabsForContentScripts(
348→    rules: ContentScriptTargetRule[],
349→    options: {
350→      preferPageReload?: boolean
351→      allowCoarseCleanup?: boolean
352→      sourceOverridesByBundleId?: Record<string, string>
353→    } = {}
354→  ): Promise<number> {
355→    if (!this.cdp || rules.length === 0) return 0
356→
357→    let reinjectedTabs = 0
358→    const targets = await this.cdp.getTargets()
359→
360→    for (const target of targets || []) {
361→      const targetType = String((target as any)?.type || '')
362→      const targetId = String((target as any)?.targetId || '')
363→      const targetUrl = String((target as any)?.url || '')
364→
365→      if (targetType !== 'page' || !targetId || !targetUrl) continue
366→      const matchingRules = rules.filter((rule) =>
367→        urlMatchesAnyContentScriptRule(targetUrl, [rule])
368→      )
369→
370→      if (matchingRules.length === 0) continue
371→
372→      try {
373→        const sessionId = await this.cdp.attachToTarget(targetId)
374→        let reinjected = false
375→
376→        if (options.preferPageReload) {
377→          reinjected = await this.reloadPageTarget(sessionId)
378→        } else {
379→          for (const [index, rule] of matchingRules.entries()) {
380→            const didReinject = await this.reinjectContentScriptRule(
381→              sessionId,
382→              rule,
383→              options.allowCoarseCleanup ?? index === 0,
384→              options.sourceOverridesByBundleId
385→            )
386→            reinjected = reinjected || didReinject
387→          }
388→
389→          if (!reinjected) {
390→            reinjected = await this.reloadPageTarget(sessionId)
391→          }
392→        }
393→
394→        if (reinjected) {
395→          reinjectedTabs += 1
396→        }
397→      } catch (error) {
398→        if (process.env.EXTENSION_AUTHOR_MODE === 'true') {
399→          console.warn(
400→            `[CDP] Failed to reinject content scripts into ${targetUrl}: ${String(
401→              (error as Error)?.message || error
402→            )}`
403→          )
404→        }
405→      }
406→    }
407→
408→    return reinjectedTabs
409→  }
410→
411→  /**
412→   * Install a persistent CDP listener that re-injects the latest content-
413→   * script bundle into any page that navigates to a URL matching a rule —
414→   * including fresh tabs, full page reloads, and same-tab URL changes.
415→   *
416→   * Chrome binds its static `content_scripts` list to the bytes it read when
417→   * the extension was loaded, and those cached bytes don't change when
418→   * Rspack rewrites the hashed bundle on edit. We can't reload the extension
419→   * without losing SW state and closing popup/sidebar. So for every matching
420→   * navigation we wait for Chrome to create the extension's isolated world
421→   * (which happens at `document_start` just before the cached content script
422→   * runs) and evaluate the fresh bundle source in that exact world. The
423→   * wrapper's `__EXTENSIONJS_DEV_REINJECT__` registry keyed on the canonical
424→   * bundle id unmounts the stale generation before mounting the new one, so
425→   * what the user sees is the latest build.
426→   *
427→   * Called on every `content-scripts` reload instruction with the latest
428→   * rules. Safe to call repeatedly — rules are simply replaced.
429→   */
430→  async registerContentScriptsForFutureNavigations(
431→    rules: ContentScriptTargetRule[]
432→  ): Promise<void> {
433→    this.activeContentScriptRules = [...rules]
434→    if (!this.cdp) return
435→
436→    if (!this.contentScriptTargetListenerInstalled) {
437→      this.contentScriptTargetListenerInstalled = true
438→      this.installContentScriptTargetListener()
439→    }
440→
441→    // Proactively attach to page targets that already match so their next
442→    // navigation (or an in-flight one) is covered without waiting for a
443→    // `targetInfoChanged` event.
444→    try {
445→      const targets = await this.cdp.getTargets()
446→      for (const target of targets || []) {
447→        if (String((target as any)?.type || '') !== 'page') continue
448→        const targetId = String((target as any)?.targetId || '')
449→        const url = String((target as any)?.url || '')
450→        if (!targetId || !url) continue
451→        if (!this.urlMatchesAnyActiveRule(url)) continue
452→        await this.ensureWatchedPageSession(targetId, url)
453→      }
454→    } catch {
455→      // Best-effort pre-attach; the listener handles future targets.
456→    }
457→  }
458→
459→  private installContentScriptTargetListener(): void {
460→    if (!this.cdp) return
461→    this.cdp.onProtocolEvent((raw: Record<string, unknown>) => {
462→      const method = String((raw as {method?: string}).method || '')
463→      const params = (raw as {params?: any}).params
464→      const sessionId = String((raw as {sessionId?: string}).sessionId || '')
465→
466→      if (
467→        method === 'Target.targetCreated' ||
468→        method === 'Target.targetInfoChanged'
469→      ) {
470→        const targetInfo = params?.targetInfo
471→        if (!targetInfo || targetInfo.type !== 'page') return
472→        const targetId = String(targetInfo.targetId || '')
473→        const url = String(targetInfo.url || '')
474→        if (!targetId) return
475→        if (!this.urlMatchesAnyActiveRule(url)) {
476→          // Update URL for tracked session so a future context created on a
477→          // now-non-matching URL is skipped.
478→          for (const info of this.watchedPageSessions.values()) {
479→            if (info.targetId === targetId) info.url = url
480→          }
481→          return
482→        }
483→        this.ensureWatchedPageSession(targetId, url).catch(() => {
484→          // Target may have closed mid-flight; ignore.
485→        })
486→        return
487→      }
488→
489→      if (method === 'Runtime.executionContextCreated') {
490→        if (!sessionId) return
491→        const session = this.watchedPageSessions.get(sessionId)
492→        if (!session) return
493→        // Initial Runtime.enable on a freshly-attached session re-emits every
494→        // existing context as if it were just created. The reinject loop has
495→        // already evaluated each rule into those contexts, so re-evaluating
496→        // here would race with the just-mounted roots — observable as MAIN
497→        // world content disappearing milliseconds after a file edit.
498→        if (this.suppressInitialContextBurst.has(sessionId)) return
499→        const context = params?.context
500→        if (!context) return
501→        this.evaluateContentScriptOnNewContext(
502→          sessionId,
503→          session,
504→          context
505→        ).catch(() => {
506→          // Best-effort; next navigation will re-try.
507→        })
508→        return
509→      }
510→
511→      if (method === 'Target.detachedFromTarget') {
512→        if (sessionId) {
513→          this.watchedPageSessions.delete(sessionId)
514→          this.releaseSessionContexts(sessionId)
515→          this.suppressInitialContextBurst.delete(sessionId)
516→        }
517→      }
518→    })
519→  }
520→
521→  private urlMatchesAnyActiveRule(url: string): boolean {
522→    if (!url || this.activeContentScriptRules.length === 0) return false
523→    return this.activeContentScriptRules.some((rule) =>
524→      urlMatchesAnyContentScriptRule(url, [rule])
525→    )
526→  }
527→
528→  private async ensureWatchedPageSession(
529→    targetId: string,
530→    url: string
531→  ): Promise<void> {
532→    if (!this.cdp) return
533→    // Already attached to this target? Update URL and bail.
534→    for (const info of this.watchedPageSessions.values()) {
535→      if (info.targetId === targetId) {
536→        info.url = url
537→        return
538→      }
539→    }
540→
541→    let sessionId: string
542→    try {
543→      sessionId = await this.cdp.attachToTarget(targetId)
544→    } catch {
545→      return
546→    }
547→    this.watchedPageSessions.set(sessionId, {targetId, url})
548→    // Suppress the executionContextCreated burst that Runtime.enable fires for
549→    // already-existing contexts — those have already been handled by the
550→    // reinject loop. Anything emitted >250ms after enable is a genuine new
551→    // context (page navigation, iframe creation) and gets the normal eval.
552→    this.suppressInitialContextBurst.add(sessionId)
553→    try {
554→      await this.cdp.sendCommand('Runtime.enable', {}, sessionId)
555→      setTimeout(() => {
556→        this.suppressInitialContextBurst.delete(sessionId)
557→      }, 250)
558→    } catch {
559→      // If Runtime.enable fails the session is likely stale; clean up.
560→      this.watchedPageSessions.delete(sessionId)
561→      this.suppressInitialContextBurst.delete(sessionId)
562→    }
563→  }
564→
565→  private async evaluateContentScriptOnNewContext(
566→    sessionId: string,
567→    session: {targetId: string; url: string},
568→    context: {
569→      id?: number
570→      origin?: string
571→      auxData?: {type?: string; isDefault?: boolean; frameId?: string}
572→    }
573→  ): Promise<void> {
574→    if (!this.cdp) return
575→    const contextId = context.id
576→    if (typeof contextId !== 'number') return
577→    if (!this.urlMatchesAnyActiveRule(session.url)) return
578→
579→    const auxData = context.auxData || {}
580→    const origin = String(context.origin || '')
581→    const extensionId =
582→      this.extensionId || (await this.deriveExtensionIdFromTargets())
583→    const extensionOrigin = extensionId
584→      ? `chrome-extension://${extensionId}`
585→      : ''
586→
587→    // Find rules that match this URL AND target the world of this context.
588→    const matchingRules = this.activeContentScriptRules.filter((rule) => {
589→      if (!urlMatchesAnyContentScriptRule(session.url, [rule])) return false
590→      if (rule.world === 'main') {
591→        return auxData.type === 'default' && auxData.isDefault === true
592→      }
593→      return auxData.type === 'isolated' && origin === extensionOrigin
594→    })
595→
596→    if (matchingRules.length === 0) return
597→
598→    for (const rule of matchingRules) {
599→      const bundlePath = resolveEmittedContentScriptFile(
600→        this.outPath,
601→        rule.index,
602→        'js'
603→      )
604→      if (!bundlePath || !fs.existsSync(bundlePath)) continue
605→      const source = this.patchReinjectSourceForInvalidatedRuntime(
606→        readTextFileCached(bundlePath)
607→      )
608→      if (!source.trim()) continue
609→      const bundleId = getCanonicalContentScriptEntryName(rule.index)
610→      // allowCoarseCleanup=true: fresh navigation, nothing to preserve.
611→      const expression = this.buildReinjectExpression(bundleId, source, true)
612→      try {
613→        await this.cdp.evaluateInContext(sessionId, expression, contextId)
614→      } catch {
615→        // Best-effort; ignore per-rule failures.
616→      }
617→    }
618→  }
619→
620→  async reinjectMatchingTabsViaExtensionRuntime(
621→    rules: ContentScriptTargetRule[]
622→  ): Promise<number> {
623→    if (!this.cdp || rules.length === 0) {
624→      this.lastRuntimeReinjectionReport = {
625→        phase: 'skipped',
626→        reason: 'missing_cdp_or_rules',
627→        hasCdp: !!this.cdp,
628→        ruleCount: rules.length
629→      }
630→      return 0
631→    }
632→
633→    const targets = await this.cdp.getTargets()
634→    const matchingUrlsByRule = new Map<number, Set<string>>()
635→
636→    for (const target of targets || []) {
637→      const targetType = String((target as any)?.type || '')
638→      const targetUrl = String((target as any)?.url || '')
639→      if (targetType !== 'page' || !targetUrl) continue
640→
641→      for (const rule of rules) {
642→        if (!urlMatchesAnyContentScriptRule(targetUrl, [rule])) continue
643→        const urls = matchingUrlsByRule.get(rule.index) || new Set<string>()
644→        urls.add(targetUrl)
645→        matchingUrlsByRule.set(rule.index, urls)
646→      }
647→    }
648→
649→    const payload = rules
650→      .map((rule) => {
651→        const jsPath = resolveEmittedContentScriptFile(
652→          this.outPath,
653→          rule.index,
654→          'js'
655→        )
656→        const cssPath = resolveEmittedContentScriptFile(
657→          this.outPath,
658→          rule.index,
659→          'css'
660→        )
661→        if (!jsPath || !fs.existsSync(jsPath)) return null
662→        const jsFile = path.relative(this.outPath, jsPath).replace(/\\/g, '/')
663→        const cssFile =
664→          cssPath && fs.existsSync(cssPath)
665→            ? path.relative(this.outPath, cssPath).replace(/\\/g, '/')
666→            : null
667→        const jsSource = readTextFileCached(jsPath)
668→        const buildTokenMatch = jsSource.match(
669→          /__EXTENSIONJS_REINJECT_BUILD_TOKEN\s*=\s*"([^"]+)"/
670→        )
671→        const proofMatch =
672→          jsSource.match(
673→            /extjs-chromium-live-content-css-modules:script-primary-\d+/
674→          ) || jsSource.match(/Live Update Proof [A-Za-z0-9_-]+/)
675→
676→        return {
677→          index: rule.index,
678→          world: rule.world,
679→          matches: [...rule.matches],
680→          urls: Array.from(matchingUrlsByRule.get(rule.index) || []).sort(),
681→          jsFile,
682→          cssFile,
683→          buildToken: buildTokenMatch?.[1] || null,
684→          proofMarker: proofMatch?.[0] || null
685→        }
686→      })
687→      .filter(
688→        (
689→          entry
690→        ): entry is {
691→          index: number
692→          world: 'extension' | 'main'
693→          matches: string[]
694→          urls: string[]
695→          jsFile: string
696→          cssFile: string | null
697→          buildToken: string | null
698→          proofMarker: string | null
699→        } => !!entry && entry.urls.length > 0
700→      )
701→
702→    if (payload.length === 0) {
703→      this.lastRuntimeReinjectionReport = {
704→        phase: 'skipped',
705→        reason: 'no_payload',
706→        ruleCount: rules.length
707→      }
708→      if (process.env.EXTENSION_AUTHOR_MODE === 'true') {
709→        console.log('[CDP] extension-runtime reinjection skipped: no payload')
710→      }
711→      return 0
712→    }
713→
714→    const runtimeTarget = await this.attachToExtensionRuntimeTarget()
715→    if (!runtimeTarget) {
716→      const targetSummary = (targets || [])
717→        .map((target) => ({
718→          type: String((target as any)?.type || ''),
719→          url: String((target as any)?.url || '')
720→        }))
721→        .filter((entry) => entry.type || entry.url)
722→      this.lastRuntimeReinjectionReport = {
723→        phase: 'unavailable',
724→        reason: 'no_runtime_target',
725→        extensionId: this.extensionId,
726→        targets: targetSummary
727→      }
728→      if (process.env.EXTENSION_AUTHOR_MODE === 'true') {
729→        console.log(
730→          `[CDP] extension-runtime reinjection unavailable: ${JSON.stringify({
731→            extensionId: this.extensionId,
732→            targets: targetSummary
733→          })}`
734→        )
735→      }
736→      return 0
737→    }
738→
739→    const result = await this.cdp.evaluate(
740→      runtimeTarget.sessionId,
741→      `(() => (async () => {
742→        const payload = ${JSON.stringify(payload)};
743→        const chromeRuntime =
744→          typeof globalThis === 'object' && globalThis && globalThis.chrome
745→            ? globalThis.chrome
746→            : null;
747→        const runtimeChrome =
748→          chromeRuntime &&
749→          chromeRuntime.scripting &&
750→          chromeRuntime.tabs
751→            ? chromeRuntime
752→            : null;
753→        if (!runtimeChrome) {
754→          return {
755→            reinjectedTabs: 0,
756→            hasRuntime: false,
757→            hasChrome: !!chromeRuntime,
758→            hasScripting: !!(chromeRuntime && chromeRuntime.scripting),
759→            hasTabs: !!(chromeRuntime && chromeRuntime.tabs),
760→            entries: payload.length,
761→            matches: []
762→          };
763→        }
764→        let reinjectedTabs = 0;
765→        const matches = [];
766→        for (const entry of payload) {
767→          const queriedTabs = await runtimeChrome.tabs.query(
768→            entry.matches.length > 0 ? { url: entry.matches } : {}
769→          );
770→          const matchingTabs = queriedTabs.filter((tab) => {
771→            if (!tab || typeof tab.id !== 'number') return false;
772→            if (typeof tab.url !== 'string' || !tab.url) return true;
773→            return entry.urls.length === 0 || entry.urls.includes(tab.url);
774→          });
775→          matches.push({
776→            index: entry.index,
777→            queriedTabs: queriedTabs.length,
778→            matchingTabs: matchingTabs.length
779→          });
780→          for (const tab of matchingTabs) {
781→            const target = { tabId: tab.id, allFrames: false };
782→            if (entry.cssFile) {
783→              try {
784→                await runtimeChrome.scripting.insertCSS({
785→                  target,
786→                  files: [entry.cssFile]
787→                });
788→              } catch (error) {}
789→            }
790→            await runtimeChrome.scripting.executeScript({
791→              target,
792→              files: [entry.jsFile],
793→              world: entry.world === 'main' ? 'MAIN' : 'ISOLATED'
794→            });
795→            reinjectedTabs += 1;
796→          }
797→        }
798→        return {
799→          reinjectedTabs,
800→          hasRuntime: true,
801→          entries: payload.length,
802→          matches
803→        };

1. **CDP targets** - `getTargets()` returns `Array<Record<string, unknown>>` but code accesses `.url`, `.type`, `.targetId` with `as any` casts

64→    // Ignore best-effort manifest introspection.
65→  }
66→
67→  const trimTrailingSep = (p: string) => {
68→    let end = p.length
69→    while (end > 0 && (p[end - 1] === '/' || p[end - 1] === '\\')) end--
70→    return p.slice(0, end)
71→  }
72→  const normalizePath = (p: string) => {
73→    try {
74→      const resolved = path.resolve(p)
75→      if (fs.existsSync(resolved)) {
76→        return trimTrailingSep(fs.realpathSync(resolved))
77→      }
78→      return trimTrailingSep(resolved)
79→    } catch {
80→      return trimTrailingSep(path.resolve(p))
81→    }
82→  }
83→
84→  const resolvedOutPath = normalizePath(outPath)
85→  const normalizedCandidates = Array.isArray(extensionPaths)
86→    ? extensionPaths.map((p) => (p ? normalizePath(p) : '')).filter(Boolean)
87→    : []
88→  const resolvedCandidates = normalizedCandidates.length
89→    ? normalizedCandidates
90→    : [resolvedOutPath]
91→  const platformIsCaseInsensitive =
92→    process.platform === 'win32' || process.platform === 'darwin'
93→  const normalizeForCompare = (p: string) =>
94→    platformIsCaseInsensitive ? p.toLowerCase() : p
95→  const matchesAnyCandidate = (p: string) => {
96→    const n = normalizeForCompare(p)
97→    return resolvedCandidates.some(
98→      (candidate) => n === normalizeForCompare(candidate)
99→    )
100→  }
101→
102→  const deriveFromProfile = () => {
103→    if (!profilePath) return null
104→
105→    const candidates: string[] = []
106→
107→    const pushPrefIfExists = (dir: string) => {
108→      const prefPath = path.join(dir, 'Preferences')
109→      if (fs.existsSync(prefPath)) candidates.push(prefPath)
110→    }
111→
112→    try {
113→      pushPrefIfExists(profilePath)
114→      pushPrefIfExists(path.join(profilePath, 'Default'))
115→      const entries = fs.readdirSync(profilePath)
116→
117→      for (const entry of entries) {
118→        if (!/^Profile\s+\d+$/i.test(entry)) continue
119→        pushPrefIfExists(path.join(profilePath, entry))
120→      }
121→    } catch {
122→      // Ignore
123→    }
124→
125→    for (const prefPath of candidates) {
126→      try {
127→        if (!fs.existsSync(prefPath)) continue
128→
129→        const prefs = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(prefPath, 'utf-8'))
130→        const settings = prefs?.extensions?.settings
131→
132→        if (!settings || typeof settings !== 'object') continue
133→
134→        const entries = Object.entries(settings) as Array<
135→          [
136→            string,
137→            {path?: string; manifest?: {name?: string; version?: string}}
138→          ]
139→        >
140→
141→        let fallbackId: string | null = null
142→
143→        for (const [id, info] of entries) {
144→          const storedPath = String(info?.path || '')
145→          if (!storedPath) continue
146→
147→          const normalized = normalizePath(storedPath)
148→
149→          if (!matchesAnyCandidate(normalized)) continue
150→
151→          const manifestName = String(info?.manifest?.name || '')
152→          const manifestVersion = String(info?.manifest?.version || '')
153→
154→          if (expectedName && manifestName === expectedName) return id
155→          if (expectedVersion && manifestVersion === expectedVersion) return id
156→
157→          fallbackId = id
158→        }
159→
160→        if (fallbackId) return fallbackId
161→      } catch {
162→        // Ignore
163→      }
164→    }
165→
166→    return null
167→  }
168→
169→  let retries = 0
170→  let deferredFirstEvalId: string | null = null
171→  let deferredUrlDerivedId: string | null = null
172→  const hasExpectedManifestIdentity = Boolean(
173→    expectedName || expectedVersion || expectedManifestVersion
174→  )
175→
176→  while (retries <= maxRetries) {
177→    try {
178→      const targets = await cdp.getTargets()
179→      const profileCandidateId = deriveFromProfile()
180→
181→      // 1) Try to derive from extension background/service worker contexts.
182→      //    Walk every candidate target before deciding — version-only matches
183→      //    are kept in a separate bucket and only applied at the END of the
184→      //    pass, so a target that matches by NAME later in the iteration
185→      //    always wins over an earlier target that matches by version alone.
186→      //    This avoids returning the wrong extension when the user's
187→      //    extension and a companion (e.g. extension-js-devtools) ship the
188→      //    same version + manifest_version
189→      let firstEvalId: string | null = null
190→      let evalIdCount = 0
191→      let urlDerivedId: string | null = null
192→      const versionFallbackIds: string[] = []
193→
194→      for (const t of targets || []) {
195→        const url: string = String((t as any)?.url || '')
196→        const type: string = String((t as any)?.type || '')
197→
198→        const typeOk = ['service_worker', 'background_page', 'worker'].includes(
199→          type
200→        )
201→
202→        if (!typeOk) continue
203→        const urlMatch = url.match(/^chrome-extension:\/\/([^\/]+)/)
204→
205→        if (!urlDerivedId && urlMatch?.[1]) {
206→          urlDerivedId = String(urlMatch[1])
207→        }
208→        if (url && !url.startsWith('chrome-extension://')) continue
209→
210→        const targetId: string | undefined = (t as any)?.targetId
211→        if (!targetId) continue
212→
213→        try {
214→          const sessionId = await cdp.attachToTarget(targetId)
215→          if (!sessionId) continue
216→
217→          await cdp.sendCommand('Runtime.enable', {}, sessionId)
218→
219→          const info = (await cdp.evaluate(
220→            sessionId,
221→            '(()=>{try{const m=chrome.runtime.getManifest?.();return {id:chrome.runtime?.id||"",name:m?.name||"",version:m?.version||"",manifestVersion:m?.manifest_version||0}}catch(_){return null}})()'
222→          )) as {
223→            id?: string
224→            name?: string
225→            version?: string
226→            manifestVersion?: number
227→          } | null
228→
229→          const id = String(info?.id || '').trim()
230→          if (!id) continue
231→
232→          evalIdCount += 1
233→
234→          if (!firstEvalId) firstEvalId = id
235→          if (profileCandidateId && id === profileCandidateId) return id
236→
237→          const gotName = String(info?.name || '')
238→          const gotVersion = String(info?.version || '')
239→          const gotManifestVersion = Number(info?.manifestVersion || 0)
240→
241→          const nameMatches =
242→            expectedName && !expectedNameIsMsg
243→              ? gotName === expectedName
244→              : false
245→          const versionMatches = expectedVersion
246→            ? gotVersion === expectedVersion
247→            : false
248→          const manifestVersionMatches = expectedManifestVersion
249→            ? gotManifestVersion === expectedManifestVersion
250→            : false
251→
252→          if (nameMatches && (!profileCandidateId || id === profileCandidateId))
253→            return id
254→
255→          if (
256→            expectedVersion &&
257→            versionMatches &&
258→            (expectedManifestVersion ? manifestVersionMatches : true) &&
259→            (!profileCandidateId || id === profileCandidateId)
260→          ) {
261→            // Defer: only fall back to a version-only match if no name match
262→            // is found in the entire pass, AND the match is unambiguous
263→            // (exactly one such candidate).
264→            if (!versionFallbackIds.includes(id)) versionFallbackIds.push(id)
265→          }
266→        } catch {
267→          // Ignore
268→        }
269→      }
270→
271→      // No name match found this pass — accept a version-only match only when
272→      // it is unique. Multiple version-only candidates means we cannot
273→      // distinguish the user extension from a sibling (for example, companion),
274→      // so fall through to the next retry / profile-derived fallback
275→      if (
276→        versionFallbackIds.length === 1 &&
277→        (expectedName ? !expectedNameIsMsg : true)
278→      ) {
279→        return versionFallbackIds[0]
280→      }
281→
282→      // With exactly one viable extension runtime, use it.
283→      if (evalIdCount === 1 && firstEvalId) {
284→        if (!hasExpectedManifestIdentity) {
285→          return firstEvalId
286→        }
287→
288→        deferredFirstEvalId = deferredFirstEvalId || firstEvalId
289→      }
290→
291→      if (profileCandidateId) {
292→        return profileCandidateId
293→      }
294→
295→      // Best-effort fallback if evaluation is blocked but URL is available.
296→      if (urlDerivedId) {
297→        if (!hasExpectedManifestIdentity) {
298→          return urlDerivedId
299→        }
300→
301→        deferredUrlDerivedId = deferredUrlDerivedId || urlDerivedId
302→      }
303→    } catch {
304→      // Ignore
305→    }
306→
307→    await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, backoffMs))
308→    retries++
309→  }
310→
311→  // Last-resort fallback after retries: prefer profile/path identity, then
312→  // evaluated runtime ID, then URL-derived ID.
313→  return deriveFromProfile() || deferredFirstEvalId || deferredUrlDerivedId
314→}

13→export async function deriveExtensionIdFromTargetsHelper(
14→  cdp: CDPClient,
15→  outPath: string,
16→  maxRetries = 6,
17→  backoffMs = 150,
18→  profilePath?: string,

20→): Promise<string | null> {

22→  let expectedName: string | undefined
23→  let expectedVersion: string | undefined
24→  let expectedManifestVersion: number | undefined
25→  let expectedNameIsMsg = false
26→
27→  try {
28→    const manifest = JSON.parse(
29→      fs.readFileSync(path.join(outPath, 'manifest.json'), 'utf-8')
30→    )

32→    expectedVersion = manifest?.version
33→    expectedManifestVersion = manifest?.manifest_version
34→    expectedNameIsMsg =
35→      typeof expectedName === 'string' && /__MSG_/i.test(expectedName)
36→
37→    // Resolve localized extension names to avoid false negatives when comparing names.
38→    if (expectedNameIsMsg) {
39→      const defaultLocale = String(manifest?.default_locale || '').trim()
40→      const msgKeyMatch = String(expectedName || '').match(/__MSG_(.+)__/i)
41→      const msgKey = msgKeyMatch ? msgKeyMatch[1] : ''
42→
43→      if (defaultLocale && msgKey) {
44→        const messagesPath = path.join(
45→          outPath,
46→          '_locales',
47→          defaultLocale,
48→          'messages.json'
49→        )
50→
51→        if (fs.existsSync(messagesPath)) {
52→          const messagesJson = JSON.parse(
53→            fs.readFileSync(messagesPath, 'utf-8')
54→          )
55→          const resolved = String(messagesJson?.[msgKey]?.message || '').trim()
56→          if (resolved) {
57→            expectedName = resolved
58→            expectedNameIsMsg = false
59→          }
60→        }
61→      }
62→    }
63→  } catch {
64→    // Ignore best-effort manifest introspection.
65→  }

68→    let end = p.length

70→    return p.slice(0, end)

72→  const normalizePath = (p: string) => {
73→    try {
74→      const resolved = path.resolve(p)

77→      }
78→      return trimTrailingSep(resolved)
79→    } catch {

81→    }

84→  const resolvedOutPath = normalizePath(outPath)
85→  const normalizedCandidates = Array.isArray(extensionPaths)

87→    : []
