90→    : [resolvedOutPath]
91→  const platformIsCaseInsensitive =
92→    process.platform === 'win32' || process.platform === 'darwin'
93→  const normalizeForCompare = (p: string) =>
94→    platformIsCaseInsensitive ? p.toLowerCase() : p
95→  const matchesAnyCandidate = (p: string) => {
96→    const n = normalizeForCompare(p)
97→    return resolvedCandidates.some(
98→      (candidate) => n === normalizeForCompare(candidate)
99→    )
100→  }
101→
102→  const deriveFromProfile = () => {
103→    if (!profilePath) return null
104→
105→    const candidates: string[] = []
106→
107→    const pushPrefIfExists = (dir: string) => {
108→      const prefPath = path.join(dir, 'Preferences')
109→      if (fs.existsSync(prefPath)) candidates.push(prefPath)
110→    }
111→
112→    try {
113→      pushPrefIfExists(profilePath)
114→      pushPrefIfExists(path.join(profilePath, 'Default'))
115→      const entries = fs.readdirSync(profilePath)
116→
117→      for (const entry of entries) {
118→        if (!/^Profile\s+\d+$/i.test(entry)) continue
119→        pushPrefIfExists(path.join(profilePath, entry))
120→      }
121→    } catch {
122→      // Ignore
123→    }
124→
125→    for (const prefPath of candidates) {
126→      try {
127→        if (!fs.existsSync(prefPath)) continue
128→
129→        const prefs = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(prefPath, 'utf-8'))
130→        const settings = prefs?.extensions?.settings
131→
132→        if (!settings || typeof settings !== 'object') continue
133→
134→        const entries = Object.entries(settings) as Array<
135→          [
136→            string,
137→            {path?: string; manifest?: {name?: string; version?: string}}
138→          ]
139→        >
140→
141→        let fallbackId: string | null = null
142→
143→        for (const [id, info] of entries) {
144→          const storedPath = String(info?.path || '')
145→          if (!storedPath) continue
146→
147→          const normalized = normalizePath(storedPath)
148→
149→          if (!matchesAnyCandidate(normalized)) continue
150→
151→          const manifestName = String(info?.manifest?.name || '')
152→          const manifestVersion = String(info?.manifest?.version || '')
153→
154→          if (expectedName && manifestName === expectedName) return id
155→          if (expectedVersion && manifestVersion === expectedVersion) return id
156→
157→          fallbackId = id
158→        }
159→
160→        if (fallbackId) return fallbackId
161→      } catch {
162→        // Ignore
163→      }
164→    }
165→
166→    return null
167→  }
168→
169→  let retries = 0
170→  let deferredFirstEvalId: string | null = null
171→  let deferredUrlDerivedId: string | null = null
172→  const hasExpectedManifestIdentity = Boolean(
173→    expectedName || expectedVersion || expectedManifestVersion
174→  )
175→
176→  while (retries <= maxRetries) {
177→    try {
178→      const targets = await cdp.getTargets()
179→      const profileCandidateId = deriveFromProfile()
180→
181→      // 1) Try to derive from extension background/service worker contexts.
182→      //    Walk every candidate target before deciding — version-only matches
183→      //    are kept in a separate bucket and only applied at the END of the
184→      //    pass, so a target that matches by NAME later in the iteration
185→      //    always wins over an earlier target that matches by version alone.
186→      //    This avoids returning the wrong extension when the user's
187→      //    extension and a companion (e.g. extension-js-devtools) ship the
188→      //    same version + manifest_version
189→      let firstEvalId: string | null = null
190→      let evalIdCount = 0
191→      let urlDerivedId: string | null = null
192→      const versionFallbackIds: string[] = []
193→
194→      for (const t of targets || []) {
195→        const url: string = String((t as any)?.url || '')
196→        const type: string = String((t as any)?.type || '')
197→
198→        const typeOk = ['service_worker', 'background_page', 'worker'].includes(
199→          type
200→        )
201→
202→        if (!typeOk) continue
203→        const urlMatch = url.match(/^chrome-extension:\/\/([^\/]+)/)
204→
205→        if (!urlDerivedId && urlMatch?.[1]) {
206→          urlDerivedId = String(urlMatch[1])
207→        }
208→        if (url && !url.startsWith('chrome-extension://')) continue
209→
210→        const targetId: string | undefined = (t as any)?.targetId
211→        if (!targetId) continue
212→
213→        try {
214→          const sessionId = await cdp.attachToTarget(targetId)
215→          if (!sessionId) continue
216→
217→          await cdp.sendCommand('Runtime.enable', {}, sessionId)
218→
219→          const info = (await cdp.evaluate(
220→            sessionId,
221→            '(()=>{try{const m=chrome.runtime.getManifest?.();return {id:chrome.runtime?.id||"",name:m?.name||"",version:m?.version||"",manifestVersion:m?.manifest_version||0}}catch(_){return null}})()'
222→          )) as {
223→            id?: string
224→            name?: string
225→            version?: string
226→            manifestVersion?: number
227→          } | null
228→
229→          const id = String(info?.id || '').trim()
230→          if (!id) continue
231→
232→          evalIdCount += 1
233→
234→          if (!firstEvalId) firstEvalId = id
235→          if (profileCandidateId && id === profileCandidateId) return id
236→
237→          const gotName = String(info?.name || '')
238→          const gotVersion = String(info?.version || '')
239→          const gotManifestVersion = Number(info?.manifestVersion || 0)
240→
241→          const nameMatches =
242→            expectedName && !expectedNameIsMsg
243→              ? gotName === expectedName
244→              : false
245→          const versionMatches = expectedVersion
246→            ? gotVersion === expectedVersion
247→            : false
248→          const manifestVersionMatches = expectedManifestVersion
249→            ? gotManifestVersion === expectedManifestVersion
250→            : false
251→
252→          if (nameMatches && (!profileCandidateId || id === profileCandidateId))
253→            return id
254→
255→          if (
256→            expectedVersion &&
257→            versionMatches &&
258→            (expectedManifestVersion ? manifestVersionMatches : true) &&
259→            (!profileCandidateId || id === profileCandidateId)
260→          ) {
261→            // Defer: only fall back to a version-only match if no name match
262→            // is found in the entire pass, AND the match is unambiguous
263→            // (exactly one such candidate).
264→            if (!versionFallbackIds.includes(id)) versionFallbackIds.push(id)
265→          }
266→        } catch {
267→          // Ignore
268→        }
269→      }
270→
271→      // No name match found this pass — accept a version-only match only when
272→      // it is unique. Multiple version-only candidates means we cannot
273→      // distinguish the user extension from a sibling (for example, companion),
274→      // so fall through to the next retry / profile-derived fallback
275→      if (
276→        versionFallbackIds.length === 1 &&
277→        (expectedName ? !expectedNameIsMsg : true)
278→      ) {
279→        return versionFallbackIds[0]
280→      }
281→
282→      // With exactly one viable extension runtime, use it.
283→      if (evalIdCount === 1 && firstEvalId) {
284→        if (!hasExpectedManifestIdentity) {

290→
291→      if (profileCandidateId) {

9→import {CDPClient} from '../cdp-client'
10→import * as messages from '../../../browsers-lib/messages'
11→
12→// Auto-enable Runtime/Log domains for attached
13→// extension sessions, and emit unified CDP log lines.
14→export function registerAutoEnableLogging(
15→  cdp: CDPClient,
16→  getExtensionId: () => string | null
17→) {
18→  cdp.onProtocolEvent((message: Record<string, unknown>) => {
19→    try {
20→      if (!message || !(message as any).method) return
21→
22→      if ((message as any).method === 'Target.attachedToTarget') {
23→        const params = (message as any).params || {}
24→        const targetInfo = params.targetInfo || {}
25→        const sessionId = params.sessionId
26→        const url: string = String(targetInfo.url || '')
27→        const type: string = String(targetInfo.type || '')
28→        const extId = getExtensionId()
29→        const matchesExtension = !!(
30→          (extId && url.includes(`chrome-extension://${extId}/`)) ||
31→          type === 'service_worker'
32→        )
33→

4→// ██╔══██╗██║   ██║██║╚██╗██║╚════╝██║     ██╔══██║██╔══██╗██║   ██║██║╚██╔╝██║██║██║   ██║██║╚██╔╝██║

6→// ╚═╝  ╚═╝ ╚═════╝ ╚═╝  ╚═══╝       ╚═════╝╚═╝  ╚═╝╚═╝  ╚═╝ ╚═════╝ ╚═╝     ╚═╝╚═╝ ╚═════╝ ╚═╝     ╚═╝
7→// MIT License (c) 2020–present Cezar Augusto — presence implies inheritance
8→
9→import {CDPClient} from '../cdp-client'
10→import * as messages from '../../../browsers-lib/messages'
11→
12→// Auto-enable Runtime/Log domains for attached
13→// extension sessions, and emit unified CDP log lines.
14→export function registerAutoEnableLogging(
15→  cdp: CDPClient,
16→  getExtensionId: () => string | null
17→) {
18→  cdp.onProtocolEvent((message: Record<string, unknown>) => {
19→    try {
20→      if (!message || !(message as any).method) return
21→
22→      if ((message as any).method === 'Target.attachedToTarget') {
23→        const params = (message as any).params || {}
24→        const targetInfo = params.targetInfo || {}
25→        const sessionId = params.sessionId
26→        const url: string = String(targetInfo.url || '')
27→        const type: string = String(targetInfo.type || '')
28→        const extId = getExtensionId()
29→        const matchesExtension = !!(
30→          (extId && url.includes(`chrome-extension://${extId}/`)) ||
31→          type === 'service_worker'
32→        )
33→
34→        if (sessionId && matchesExtension) {
35→          // Enable runtime and log domains for this session
36→          cdp.sendCommand('Runtime.enable', {}, sessionId).catch(() => {})
37→          cdp.sendCommand('Log.enable', {}, sessionId).catch(() => {})
38→        }
39→      } else if (
40→        (message as any).method === 'Runtime.consoleAPICalled' ||
41→        (message as any).method === 'Log.entryAdded'
42→      ) {
43→        // Only print unified CDP logs when user has enabled verbose output
44→        if (String(process.env.EXTENSION_VERBOSE || '').trim() === '1') {
45→          const ts = new Date().toISOString()
46→          console.log(
47→            messages.cdpUnifiedExtensionLog(ts, (message as any).params)
48→          )
49→        }
50→      }
51→    } catch (error: unknown) {
52→      if (process.env.EXTENSION_AUTHOR_MODE === 'true') {
53→        console.warn(
54→          messages.cdpProtocolEventHandlerError(
55→            String((error as Error)?.message || error)
56→          )
57→        )
58→      }
59→    }
60→  })
61→}

1→// ██████╗ ██╗   ██╗███╗   ██╗       ██████╗██╗  ██╗██████╗  ██████╗ ███╗   ███╗██╗██╗   ██╗███╗   ███╗
2→// ██╔══██╗██║   ██║████╗  ██║      ██╔════╝██║  ██║██╔══██╗██╔═══██╗████╗ ████║██║██║   ██║████╗ ████║
3→// ██████╔╝██║   ██║██╔██╗ ██║█████╗██║     ███████║██████╔╝██║   ██║██╔████╔██║██║██║   ██║██╔████╔██║
4→// ██╔══██╗██║   ██║██║╚██╗██║╚════╝██║     ██╔══██║██╔══██╗██║   ██║██║╚██╔╝██║██║██║   ██║██║╚██╔╝██║
5→// ██║  ██║╚██████╔╝██║ ╚████║      ╚██████╗██║  ██║██║  ██║╚██████╔╝██║ ╚═╝ ██║██║╚██████╔╝██║ ╚═╝ ██║
6→// ╚═╝  ╚═╝ ╚═════╝ ╚═╝  ╚═══╝       ╚═════╝╚═╝  ╚═╝╚═╝  ╚═╝ ╚═════╝ ╚═╝     ╚═╝╚═╝ ╚═════╝ ╚═╝     ╚═╝
7→// MIT License (c) 2020–present Cezar Augusto — presence implies inheritance
8→
9→import colors from 'pintor'
10→import type {
11→  Controller,
12→  ChromiumLogger,
13→  CdpEvent,
14→  ConsoleAPICalledEvent,
15→  LogEntryAddedEvent,
16→  LogLevel
17→} from '../chromium-types'
18→
19→export async function setupUnifiedLogging(
20→  controller: Controller,
21→  opts: ChromiumLogger
22→) {
23→  const level = String(opts.level || '').toLowerCase()
24→  if (!level || level === 'off') return
25→
26→  const colorOn = opts?.color !== false
27→  const c = {
28→    gray: (s: string) => (colorOn && colors.gray ? colors.gray(s) : s),
29→    red: (s: string) => (colorOn && colors.red ? colors.red(s) : s),
30→    yellow: (s: string) =>
31→      colorOn && colors.brightYellow ? colors.brightYellow(s) : s,
32→    cyan: (s: string) => (colorOn && colors.cyan ? colors.cyan(s) : s),
33→    magenta: (s: string) => (colorOn && colors.magenta ? colors.magenta(s) : s),
34→    white: (s: string) => (colorOn && colors.white ? colors.white(s) : s)
35→  }
36→
37→  await controller.enableUnifiedLogging({
38→    level: level as LogLevel,
39→    contexts: opts.contexts,
40→    urlFilter: opts.urlFilter,
41→    tabFilter: opts.tabFilter
42→  })
43→
44→  const recentKeys = new Map<string, number>()
45→  const DEDUPE_MS = 1000
46→
47→  const subscribe = controller.onProtocolEvent
48→
49→  if (!subscribe) return
50→
51→  subscribe((rawEvt) => {
52→    try {
53→      const evt = rawEvt as CdpEvent
54→      const showTs = opts.timestamps !== false
55→      const method = evt?.method
56→      let level = 'info'
57→      let text = ''
58→      let url = ''
59→      let line = 0
60→      let col = 0
61→
62→      if (method === 'Runtime.consoleAPICalled') {
63→        const p = (evt as ConsoleAPICalledEvent).params || {}
64→        level = String(p.type || 'log').toLowerCase()
65→        const args = p.args || []
66→        const first = args[0]
67→        text = String(
68→          (first && ((first as any).value || (first as any).description)) || ''
69→        )
70→        const loc = p.stackTrace?.callFrames?.[0]
71→
72→        if (loc) {
73→          url = String(loc.url || '')
74→          line = Number(loc.lineNumber || 0)
75→          col = Number(loc.columnNumber || 0)
76→        }
77→      } else if (method === 'Log.entryAdded') {
78→        const entry = (evt as LogEntryAddedEvent).params?.entry
79→        if (!entry) return
80→
81→        level = String(entry.level || 'info').toLowerCase()
82→        text = String(entry.text || '')
83→        url = String(entry.url || '')
84→        line = Number(entry.lineNumber || 0)
85→        col = Number(entry.columnNumber || 0)
86→      } else {
87→        return
88→      }
89→
90→      // Granular context mapping aligned with centralized DevTools UI
91→      const context = (() => {
92→        const u = String(url || '')
93→        if (u.startsWith('chrome-extension://')) {
94→          // Recognize emitted content script artefacts under extension URLs
95→          if (
96→            /\bcontent_scripts\/content-\d+\.js\b/i.test(u) ||
97→            /\b\/content\/script/i.test(u)
98→          )
99→            return 'content'
100→          if (/\bbackground\//i.test(u) || /\bservice_worker\.js$/i.test(u))
101→            return 'background'
102→          if (/\bdevtools\//i.test(u)) return 'devtools'
103→          if (/\boptions\//i.test(u)) return 'options'
104→          if (/(^|\/)action\//i.test(u) || /\bpopup\//i.test(u)) return 'popup'
105→          if (/\b(sidebar|side_panel)\//i.test(u)) return 'sidebar'
106→          return 'background'
107→        }
108→
109→        // Heuristic for content scripts when URL points to page but content artefacts mentioned
110→        if (/\bcontent(_scripts)?\b/i.test(u) || /\bcontent[-_/]/i.test(text)) {
111→          return 'content'
112→        }
113→
114→        return 'page'
115→      })()
116→
117→      const prefix = '⏵⏵⏵'
118→
119→      function fmtLevel(lvl: string) {
120→        const up = String(lvl || 'log').toUpperCase()
121→        switch (String(lvl || '').toLowerCase()) {
122→          case 'error':
123→            return c.red(up)
124→          case 'warn':
125→            return c.yellow(up)
126→          case 'info':
127→            return c.cyan(up)
128→          case 'debug':
129→            return c.magenta(up)
130→          case 'trace':
131→            return c.white(up)
132→          case 'log':
133→          default:
134→            return c.gray(up)
135→        }
136→      }
137→
138→      const where = url ? `${url}:${line}:${col}` : ''
139→      const msg = text && text.trim().length ? text.trim() : '(none)'
140→
141→      const key = `${method}|${level}|${context}|${where}|${msg}`
142→      const nowMs = Date.now()
143→      const last = recentKeys.get(key) || 0
144→
145→      if (nowMs - last < DEDUPE_MS) return
146→
147→      recentKeys.set(key, nowMs)
148→
149→      if (recentKeys.size > 500) {
150→        const cutoff = nowMs - DEDUPE_MS
151→        for (const [k, t] of recentKeys) {
152→          if (t < cutoff) {
153→            recentKeys.delete(k)
154→          }
155→        }
156→      }
157→
158→      const nowDate = new Date()
159→      const tsIso = showTs ? `${nowDate.toISOString()} ` : ''
160→
161→      // JSON/NDJSON aligned output for machine parsing
162→      if (String(opts.format || '').toLowerCase() !== 'pretty') {
163→        const event = {
164→          timestamp: nowDate.toISOString(),
165→          level: String(level || 'log'),
166→          context,
167→          message: msg,
168→          url,
169→          line,
170→          column: col,
171→          tabId: opts.tabFilter ?? undefined
172→        }
173→
174→        try {
175→          // For both json and ndjson we print one object per line
176→          console.log(JSON.stringify(event))
177→        } catch {
178→          // fallback to plain
179→          console.log(
180→            `${prefix} ${level} ${context} - ${msg}${where ? ' (' + where + ')' : ''}`
181→          )
182→        }
183→        return
184→      }
185→
186→      // Pretty human output mirroring DevTools, Vercel tone
187→      const ctx = c.gray(`[${context}]`)
188→      const lvl = fmtLevel(level)
189→      const whereStr = where ? ` ${c.gray(where)}` : ''
190→      const message = msg && msg.trim().length ? msg.trim() : '(none)'
191→      console.log(`${tsIso}${prefix} ${ctx} ${lvl}${whereStr} - ${message}`)
192→    } catch {
193→      // Ignore
194→    }
195→  })
196→}
197→

129→            return c.magenta(up)
130→          case 'trace':
131→            return c.white(up)
132→          case 'log':
133→          default:
134→            return c.gray(up)
135→        }
136→      }
137→
138→      const where = url ? `${url}:${line}:${col}` : ''
139→      const msg = text && text.trim().length ? text.trim() : '(none)'
140→
141→      const key = `${method}|${level}|${context}|${where}|${msg}`
142→      const nowMs = Date.now()
143→      const last = recentKeys.get(key) || 0
144→
145→      if (nowMs - last < DEDUPE_MS) return
146→
147→      recentKeys.set(key, nowMs)
148→
149→      if (recentKeys.size > 500) {
150→        const cutoff = nowMs - DEDUPE_MS
151→        for (const [k, t] of recentKeys) {
152→          if (t < cutoff) {
153→            recentKeys.delete(k)
154→          }
155→        }
156→      }
157→
158→      const nowDate = new Date()
159→      const tsIso = showTs ? `${nowDate.toISOString()} ` : ''
160→
161→      // JSON/NDJSON aligned output for machine parsing
162→      if (String(opts.format || '').toLowerCase() !== 'pretty') {
163→        const event = {
164→          timestamp: nowDate.toISOString(),
165→          level: String(level || 'log'),
166→          context,
167→          message: msg,
168→          url,
169→          line,
170→          column: col,
171→          tabId: opts.tabFilter ?? undefined
172→        }
173→
174→        try {
175→          // For both json and ndjson we print one object per line
176→          console.log(JSON.stringify(event))
177→        } catch {
178→          // fallback to plain
179→          console.log(
180→            `${prefix} ${level} ${context} - ${msg}${where ? ' (' + where + ')' : ''}`
181→          )
182→        }
183→        return
184→      }
185→
186→      // Pretty human output mirroring DevTools, Vercel tone
187→      const ctx = c.gray(`[${context}]`)
188→      const lvl = fmtLevel(level)
189→      const whereStr = where ? ` ${c.gray(where)}` : ''
190→      const message = msg && msg.trim().length ? msg.trim() : '(none)'
191→      console.log(`${tsIso}${prefix} ${ctx} ${lvl}${whereStr} - ${message}`)
192→    } catch {
193→      // Ignore
194→    }
195→  })
196→}
197→

4→// ██╔══██╗██║   ██║██║╚██╗██║╚════╝██║     ██╔══██║██╔══██╗██║   ██║██║╚██╔╝██║██║██║   ██║██║╚██╔╝██║

6→// ╚═╝  ╚═╝ ╚═════╝ ╚═╝  ╚═══╝       ╚═════╝╚═╝  ╚═╝╚═╝  ╚═╝ ╚═════╝ ╚═╝     ╚═╝╚═╝ ╚═════╝ ╚═╝     ╚═╝
7→// MIT License (c) 2020–present Cezar Augusto — presence implies inheritance
8→
9→import colors from 'pintor'
10→import type {
11→  Controller,
12→  ChromiumLogger,
13→  CdpEvent,
14→  ConsoleAPICalledEvent,
15→  LogEntryAddedEvent,
16→  LogLevel
17→} from '../chromium-types'
18→
19→export async function setupUnifiedLogging(
20→  controller: Controller,
21→  opts: ChromiumLogger
22→) {
23→  const level = String(opts.level || '').toLowerCase()
24→  if (!level || level === 'off') return
25→
26→  const colorOn = opts?.color !== false
27→  const c = {
28→    gray: (s: string) => (colorOn && colors.gray ? colors.gray(s) : s),
29→    red: (s: string) => (colorOn && colors.red ? colors.red(s) : s),
30→    yellow: (s: string) =>
31→      colorOn && colors.brightYellow ? colors.brightYellow(s) : s,
32→    cyan: (s: string) => (colorOn && colors.cyan ? colors.cyan(s) : s),
33→    magenta: (s: string) => (colorOn && colors.magenta ? colors.magenta(s) : s),
34→    white: (s: string) => (colorOn && colors.white ? colors.white(s) : s)
35→  }
36→
37→  await controller.enableUnifiedLogging({
38→    level: level as LogLevel,
39→    contexts: opts.contexts,
40→    urlFilter: opts.urlFilter,
41→    tabFilter: opts.tabFilter
42→  })
43→
44→  const recentKeys = new Map<string, number>()
45→  const DEDUPE_MS = 1000
46→
47→  const subscribe = controller.onProtocolEvent
48→
49→  if (!subscribe) return
50→
51→  subscribe((rawEvt) => {
52→    try {
53→      const evt = rawEvt as CdpEvent
54→      const showTs = opts.timestamps !== false
55→      const method = evt?.method
56→      let level = 'info'
57→      let text = ''
58→      let url = ''
59→      let line = 0
60→      let col = 0
61→
62→      if (method === 'Runtime.consoleAPICalled') {
63→        const p = (evt as ConsoleAPICalledEvent).params || {}
64→        level = String(p.type || 'log').toLowerCase()
65→        const args = p.args || []
66→        const first = args[0]
67→        text = String(
68→          (first && ((first as any).value || (first as any).description)) || ''
69→        )
70→        const loc = p.stackTrace?.callFrames?.[0]
71→
72→        if (loc) {
73→          url = String(loc.url || '')
74→          line = Number(loc.lineNumber || 0)
75→          col = Number(loc.columnNumber || 0)
76→        }
77→      } else if (method === 'Log.entryAdded') {
78→        const entry = (evt as LogEntryAddedEvent).params?.entry
79→        if (!entry) return
80→
81→        level = String(entry.level || 'info').toLowerCase()
82→        text = String(entry.text || '')
83→        url = String(entry.url || '')
84→        line = Number(entry.lineNumber || 0)
85→        col = Number(entry.columnNumber || 0)
86→      } else {
87→        return
88→      }
89→
90→      // Granular context mapping aligned with centralized DevTools UI
91→      const context = (() => {
92→        const u = String(url || '')
93→        if (u.startsWith('chrome-extension://')) {
94→          // Recognize emitted content script artefacts under extension URLs
95→          if (
96→            /\bcontent_scripts\/content-\d+\.js\b/i.test(u) ||
97→            /\b\/content\/script/i.test(u)
98→          )
99→            return 'content'
100→          if (/\bbackground\//i.test(u) || /\bservice_worker\.js$/i.test(u))
101→            return 'background'
102→          if (/\bdevtools\//i.test(u)) return 'devtools'
103→          if (/\boptions\//i.test(u)) return 'options'
104→          if (/(^|\/)action\//i.test(u) || /\bpopup\//i.test(u)) return 'popup'
105→          if (/\b(sidebar|side_panel)\//i.test(u)) return 'sidebar'
106→          return 'background'
107→        }
108→
109→        // Heuristic for content scripts when URL points to page but content artefacts mentioned
110→        if (/\bcontent(_scripts)?\b/i.test(u) || /\bcontent[-_/]/i.test(text)) {
111→          return 'content'
112→        }
113→
114→        return 'page'
115→      })()
116→
117→      const prefix = '⏵⏵⏵'
118→
119→      function fmtLevel(lvl: string) {
120→        const up = String(lvl || 'log').toUpperCase()
121→        switch (String(lvl || '').toLowerCase()) {
122→          case 'error':
123→            return c.red(up)
124→          case 'warn':
125→            return c.yellow(up)
126→          case 'info':
127→            return c.cyan(up)
128→          case 'debug':
129→            return c.magenta(up)
130→          case 'trace':
131→            return c.white(up)
132→          case 'log':
133→          default:
134→            return c.gray(up)
135→        }
136→      }
137→
138→      const where = url ? `${url}:${line}:${col}` : ''
139→      const msg = text && text.trim().length ? text.trim() : '(none)'
140→
141→      const key = `${method}|${level}|${context}|${where}|${msg}`
142→      const nowMs = Date.now()
143→      const last = recentKeys.get(key) || 0
144→
145→      if (nowMs - last < DEDUPE_MS) return
146→
147→      recentKeys.set(key, nowMs)
148→
149→      if (recentKeys.size > 500) {
150→        const cutoff = nowMs - DEDUPE_MS
151→        for (const [k, t] of recentKeys) {
152→          if (t < cutoff) {
153→            recentKeys.delete(k)
154→          }
155→        }
156→      }
157→
158→      const nowDate = new Date()
159→      const tsIso = showTs ? `${nowDate.toISOString()} ` : ''
160→
161→      // JSON/NDJSON aligned output for machine parsing
162→      if (String(opts.format || '').toLowerCase() !== 'pretty') {
163→        const event = {
164→          timestamp: nowDate.toISOString(),
165→          level: String(level || 'log'),
166→          context,
167→          message: msg,
168→          url,
169→          line,
170→          column: col,
171→          tabId: opts.tabFilter ?? undefined
172→        }
173→
174→        try {
175→          // For both json and ndjson we print one object per line
176→          console.log(JSON.stringify(event))
177→        } catch {
178→          // fallback to plain
179→          console.log(
180→            `${prefix} ${level} ${context} - ${msg}${where ? ' (' + where + ')' : ''}`
181→          )
182→        }
183→        return
184→      }
185→
186→      // Pretty human output mirroring DevTools, Vercel tone
187→      const ctx = c.gray(`[${context}]`)
188→      const lvl = fmtLevel(level)
189→      const whereStr = where ? ` ${c.gray(where)}` : ''
190→      const message = msg && msg.trim().length ? msg.trim() : '(none)'
191→      console.log(`${tsIso}${prefix} ${ctx} ${lvl}${whereStr} - ${message}`)
192→    } catch {
193→      // Ignore
194→    }
195→  })
196→}
197→

9→import type {ChromiumContext} from '../chromium-context'
10→import {setupUnifiedLogging} from './unified-logging'
11→
12→/**
13→ * ChromiumUnifiedLoggerPlugin
14→ *
15→ * Intended responsibilities:
16→ * - Wait for controller readiness; enable unified CDP logging
17→ * - Respect level/contexts/filters/format/timestamps/color
18→ */
19→export class ChromiumUnifiedLoggerPlugin {
20→  constructor(
21→    private readonly options: {
22→      logLevel?: string
23→      logContexts?: string[]
24→      logUrl?: string
25→      logTab?: number | string
26→      logFormat?: 'pretty' | 'json' | 'ndjson'
27→      logTimestamps?: boolean
28→      logColor?: boolean
29→    },
30→    private readonly ctx: ChromiumContext
31→  ) {
32→    // Wiring will be introduced in a follow-up edit.
33→  }
34→
35→  apply(_compiler: any) {
36→    const lvl = String(this.options?.logLevel || '').toLowerCase()
37→
38→    if (!lvl || lvl === 'off') return
39→
40→    this.ctx.onControllerReady(async (controller) => {
41→      try {
42→        await setupUnifiedLogging(controller, {
43→          level: this.options?.logLevel,
44→          contexts: this.options?.logContexts,
45→          urlFilter: this.options?.logUrl,
46→          tabFilter: this.options?.logTab,
47→          format: this.options?.logFormat,
48→          timestamps: this.options?.logTimestamps !== false,
49→          color: this.options?.logColor !== false
50→        })
51→      } catch {
52→        // best-effort
53→      }
54→    })
55→  }
56→}

9→// Browser type union — mirrors the develop package's DevOptions['browser']
10→// but defined locally so this package has zero bundler dependencies.
11→export type BrowserType =
12→  | 'chrome'
13→  | 'edge'
14→  | 'firefox'
15→  | 'chromium'
16→  | 'chromium-based'
17→  | 'gecko-based'
18→  | 'firefox-based'

20→  | 'webkit-based'

22→/**
23→ * Lightweight stand-in for rspack's Compilation used by browser-launching code.
24→ * Only the properties actually read by launch/reload/inspection flows are declared.
25→ */
26→export interface CompilationLike {
27→  options: {
28→    mode?: string
29→    output?: {path?: string}
30→    context?: string
31→  }
32→  outputOptions?: {path?: string}
33→  getAsset?: (name: string) => {source: {source: () => string}} | undefined
34→}
35→
36→/**
37→ * Minimal logger interface mirroring the shape returned by
38→ * rspack's `compiler.getInfrastructureLogger()`.
39→ */
40→export interface BrowserLogger {
41→  info: (...args: unknown[]) => void
42→  warn: (...args: unknown[]) => void
43→  error: (...args: unknown[]) => void
44→  debug: (...args: unknown[]) => void
45→}

58→/**
59→ * List of default browser flags used by extension.js.
60→ * These can be excluded using the `excludeBrowserFlags` option.

62→ * Each flag disables or modifies a specific browser feature for a more controlled development environment.

65→  | '--no-first-run' // Disable Chrome's native first run experience
66→  | '--disable-client-side-phishing-detection' // Disable client-side phishing detection
67→  | '--disable-component-extensions-with-background-pages' // Disable some built-in extensions not affected by '--disable-extensions'
68→  | '--disable-default-apps' // Disable installation of default apps
69→  | '--disable-features=InterestFeedContentSuggestions' // Disable the Discover feed on NTP
70→  | '--disable-features=Translate' // Disable Chrome translation
71→  | '--hide-scrollbars' // Hide scrollbars from screenshots
72→  | '--mute-audio' // Mute all audio in the browser
73→  | '--no-default-browser-check' // Disable the default browser check
74→  | '--ash-no-nudges' // Avoid blue bubble "user education" nudges
75→  | '--disable-search-engine-choice-screen' // Disable the 2023+ search engine choice screen
76→  | '--disable-features=MediaRoute' // Disable Chrome Media Router
77→  | '--use-mock-keychain' // Use mock keychain on Mac to prevent permissions dialog
78→  | '--disable-background-networking' // Disable various background network services
79→  | '--disable-breakpad' // Disable crashdump collection
80→  | '--disable-component-update' // Don't update browser components
81→  | '--disable-domain-reliability' // Disable Domain Reliability Monitoring
82→  | '--disable-features=AutofillServerCommunicatio' // Disable autofill server communication
83→  | '--disable-features=CertificateTransparencyComponentUpdate' // Disable certificate transparency component updates
84→  | '--disable-sync' // Disable syncing to a Google account
85→  | '--disable-features=OptimizationHints' // Disable the Chrome Optimization Guide
86→  | '--disable-features=DialMediaRouteProvider' // Disable the MediaRouter feature (lighter version)
87→  | '--no-pings' // Don't send hyperlink auditing pings
88→  | '--enable-features=SidePanelUpdates' // Ensure the side panel is visible for testing
89→  | '--enable-unsafe-extension-debugging' // Allow CDP-based extension management (Chrome 126+)
90→  | '--silent-debugger-extension-api' // Suppress the "X is debugging this browser" infobar
91→
92→/**
93→ * Options for the browser plugin.
94→ */
95→export interface PluginOptions {
96→  /**
97→   * Do not open the browser automatically after launch.
98→   * @default false
99→   */
100→  noOpen?: boolean
101→
102→  /**
103→   * Additional browser flags to pass to the browser process.
104→   * Example: ['--disable-extensions', '--disable-gpu']
105→   */
106→  browserFlags?: string[]
107→
108→  /**
109→   * Array of browser flags to exclude from the default set.
110→   * Example: ['--hide-scrollbars', '--mute-audio']
111→   */
112→  excludeBrowserFlags?: Array<DefaultBrowserFlags | string>
113→
114→  /**
115→   * Path to the browser profile directory, or false for a temporary profile.
116→   * Example: 'dist/extension' or false
117→   */
118→  profile?: string | false
119→
120→  /**
121→   * Use a persistent managed profile for development.
122→   * Defaults to false (ephemeral temp profiles are used).
123→   */
124→  persistProfile?: boolean
125→
126→  /**
127→   * Browser preferences object.
128→   * Example: { extensions: ['dist/extension', 'dist/extension2'] }
129→   */
130→  preferences?: Record<string, unknown>
131→
132→  /**
133→   * Firefox only: persist changes made to the profile directory.
134→   */
135→  keepProfileChanges?: boolean
136→
137→  /**
138→   * Firefox only: copy an existing profile before launching.
139→   */
140→  copyFromProfile?: string
141→
142→  /**
143→   * URL to open when the browser starts.
144→   * Example: 'http://localhost:3000'
145→   */
146→  startingUrl?: string
147→
148→  /**
149→   * Enable the browser console.
150→   * @default false
151→   */
152→  browserConsole?: boolean
153→
154→  /**
155→   * Open DevTools automatically.
156→   * @default false
157→   */
158→  devtools?: boolean
159→
160→  /**
161→   * Path to the Chromium binary.
162→   * Example: '/path/to/chromium'
163→   */
164→  chromiumBinary?: string
165→
166→  /**
167→   * Path to the Gecko (Firefox) binary.
168→   * Example: '/path/to/gecko'
169→   */
170→  geckoBinary?: string
171→}
172→
173→/**
174→ * Main interface for the browser plugin.
175→ */
176→export interface PluginInterface extends PluginOptions {
177→  /**
178→   * Browser type to launch.
179→   * @default 'chrome'
180→   * @see DevOptions['browser']
181→   * Example: 'chrome' | 'edge' | 'firefox'
182→   */
183→  browser: BrowserType
184→
185→  /**
186→   * Path(s) to the extension(s) to load.
187→   * Example: 'dist/extension' or ['dist/extension', 'dist/extension2']
188→   */
189→  extension: string | string[]
190→
191→  /**
192→   * Port to use for the extension or debugging.
193→   * Example: 12345 or '12345'
194→   */
195→  port?: string | number
196→
197→  /**
198→   * Internal auto-generated instance ID (not user-configurable).
199→   * Example: '1234567890'
200→   */
201→  instanceId?: string
202→
203→  /**
204→   * Path to source files for inspection.
205→   * Example: 'dist/extension'
206→   */
207→  source?: string
208→
209→  /**
210→   * Whether to watch the source directory for changes.
211→   * @default false
212→   */
213→  watchSource?: boolean
214→
215→  /**
216→   * Output format for source inspection HTML.
217→   */
218→  sourceFormat?: 'pretty' | 'json' | 'ndjson'
219→
220→  /**
221→   * Output a compact summary instead of full HTML.
222→   * @default false
223→   */
224→  sourceSummary?: boolean
225→
226→  /**
227→   * Output page metadata (readyState, viewport, frames).
228→   */
229→  sourceMeta?: boolean
230→
231→  /**
232→   * CSS selectors to probe for debugging.
233→   */
234→  sourceProbe?: string[]
235→
236→  /**
237→   * Output a compact extension root tree.
238→   */
239→  sourceTree?: 'off' | 'root-only'
240→
241→  /**
242→   * Output console summary (best-effort).
243→   */
244→  sourceConsole?: boolean
245→
246→  /**
247→   * Output DOM snapshots and diffs.
248→   */
249→  sourceDom?: boolean
250→
251→  /**
252→   * Maximum HTML bytes to print (0 disables truncation).
253→   */
254→  sourceMaxBytes?: number
255→
256→  /**
257→   * Redaction mode for HTML output.
258→   */
259→  sourceRedact?: 'off' | 'safe' | 'strict'
260→
261→  /**
262→   * Control Shadow DOM inclusion when extracting HTML.
263→   */
264→  sourceIncludeShadow?: 'off' | 'open-only' | 'all'
265→
266→  /**
267→   * Include diff metadata on watch updates.
268→   */
269→  sourceDiff?: boolean
270→
271→  /**
272→   * Log level for unified logger (Chromium CDP logging).
273→   * One of: 'off', 'error', 'warn', 'info', 'debug', 'trace', 'all'
274→   */
275→  logLevel?: 'off' | 'error' | 'warn' | 'info' | 'debug' | 'trace' | 'all'
276→
277→  /**
278→   * Log contexts to enable.
279→   * Example: ['background', 'content', 'page']
280→   */
281→  logContexts?: Array<
282→    | 'background'
283→    | 'content'
284→    | 'page'
285→    | 'sidebar'
286→    | 'popup'
287→    | 'options'
288→    | 'devtools'
289→  >
290→
291→  /**
292→   * Log output format.
293→   * One of: 'pretty', 'json'
294→   */
295→  logFormat?: 'pretty' | 'json' | 'ndjson'
296→
297→  /**
298→   * Include timestamps in logs.
299→   * @default true
300→   */
301→  logTimestamps?: boolean
302→
303→  /**
304→   * Enable colored log output.
305→   * @default false
306→   */
307→  logColor?: boolean
308→
309→  /**
310→   * URL to send logs to.
311→   * Example: 'http://localhost:3000'
312→   */
313→  logUrl?: string
314→
315→  /**
316→   * Tab ID or index for logging context.
317→   */
318→  logTab?: number | string
319→
320→  /**
321→   * Perform a dry run without launching the browser.
322→   * @default false
323→   */
324→  dryRun?: boolean
325→}
326→
327→/**
328→ * Runtime state shared with post-launch setup helpers (CDP/RDP).
329→ * Extends the main PluginInterface with transient fields used during runtime.
330→ */
331→export interface PluginRuntime extends PluginInterface {
332→  // Intentionally empty for shared runtime; per-run runtime fields live in
333→  // run-chromium/chromium-types.ts and run-firefox/firefox-types.ts
334→}
335→
336→export type LogLevel =
337→  | 'off'
338→  | 'error'
339→  | 'warn'
340→  | 'info'
341→  | 'debug'
342→  | 'trace'
343→  | 'all'
344→
345→export type LogContext =
346→  | 'background'
347→  | 'content'
348→  | 'page'
349→  | 'sidebar'
350→  | 'popup'
351→  | 'options'
352→  | 'devtools'
353→
354→export type LogFormat = 'pretty' | 'json' | 'ndjson'
355→
356→/**
357→ * Unified controller interface used by post-launch logging flows.
358→ * Implemented by both CDP and RDP controllers.
359→ */
360→export interface Controller {
361→  enableUnifiedLogging: (opts: {
362→    level?: string
363→    contexts?: string[] | undefined
364→    urlFilter?: string | undefined
365→    tabFilter?: number | string | undefined
366→    format?: 'pretty' | 'json' | 'ndjson'
367→    timestamps?: boolean
368→    color?: boolean
369→  }) => Promise<void>
370→  /**
371→   * Optional event subscription hook for protocol events (CDP/RDP).
372→   * Present in Chromium CDP flows; Firefox may omit it.
373→   */
374→  onProtocolEvent?: (
375→    cb: (evt: {method?: string; params?: unknown}) => void
376→  ) => void
377→}

// ██╔══██╗██╔══██╗██║   ██║██║███╗██║╚════██║██╔══╝  ██╔══██╗╚════██║
// ██████╔╝██║  ██║╚██████╔╝╚███╔███╔╝███████║███████╗██║  ██║███████║
// ╚═════╝ ╚═╝  ╚═╝ ╚═════╝  ╚══╝╚══╝ ╚══════╝╚══════╝╚═╝  ╚═╝╚══════╝
// MIT License (c) 2020–present Cezar Augusto — presence implies inheritance

// Browser type union — mirrors the develop package's DevOptions['browser']
// but defined locally so this package has zero bundler dependencies.
export type BrowserType =
  | 'chrome'
  | 'edge'
  | 'firefox'
  | 'chromium'
  | 'chromium-based'
  | 'gecko-based'
  | 'firefox-based'
  | 'safari'
  | 'webkit-based'

/**
 * Lightweight stand-in for rspack's Compilation used by browser-launching code.
 * Only the properties actually read by launch/reload/inspection flows are declared.
 */
export interface CompilationLike {
  options: {
    mode?: string
    output?: {path?: string}
    context?: string
  }
  outputOptions?: {path?: string}
  getAsset?: (name: string) => {source: {source: () => string}} | undefined
}

/**
 * Minimal logger interface mirroring the shape returned by
 * rspack's `compiler.getInfrastructureLogger()`.
 */
export interface BrowserLogger {
  info: (...args: unknown[]) => void
  warn: (...args: unknown[]) => void
  error: (...args: unknown[]) => void
  debug: (...args: unknown[]) => void
}

/**
 * Stand-in for rspack's BrowserConfig — the subset used by browser-config helpers.
 */
export interface BrowserConfig {
  extensionPath: string
  profilePath: string | false
  preferences: Record<string, unknown>
  browserFlags: string[]
  startingUrl: string | undefined
}

/**
 * List of default browser flags used by extension.js.
 * These can be excluded using the `excludeBrowserFlags` option.
 *
 * Each flag disables or modifies a specific browser feature for a more controlled development environment.
 */
export type DefaultBrowserFlags =
  | '--no-first-run' // Disable Chrome's native first run experience
  | '--disable-client-side-phishing-detection' // Disable client-side phishing detection
  | '--disable-component-extensions-with-background-pages' // Disable some built-in extensions not affected by '--disable-extensions'
  | '--disable-default-apps' // Disable installation of default apps
  | '--disable-features=InterestFeedContentSuggestions' // Disable the Discover feed on NTP
  | '--disable-features=Translate' // Disable Chrome translation
  | '--hide-scrollbars' // Hide scrollbars from screenshots
  | '--mute-audio' // Mute all audio in the browser
  | '--no-default-browser-check' // Disable the default browser check
  | '--ash-no-nudges' // Avoid blue bubble "user education" nudges
  | '--disable-search-engine-choice-screen' // Disable the 2023+ search engine choice screen
  | '--disable-features=MediaRoute' // Disable Chrome Media Router
  | '--use-mock-keychain' // Use mock keychain on Mac to prevent permissions dialog
  | '--disable-background-networking' // Disable various background network services
  | '--disable-breakpad' // Disable crashdump collection
  | '--disable-component-update' // Don't update browser components
  | '--disable-domain-reliability' // Disable Domain Reliability Monitoring
  | '--disable-features=AutofillServerCommunicatio' // Disable autofill server communication
  | '--disable-features=CertificateTransparencyComponentUpdate' // Disable certificate transparency component updates
  | '--disable-sync' // Disable syncing to a Google account
  | '--disable-features=OptimizationHints' // Disable the Chrome Optimization Guide
  | '--disable-features=DialMediaRouteProvider' // Disable the MediaRouter feature (lighter version)
  | '--no-pings' // Don't send hyperlink auditing pings
  | '--enable-features=SidePanelUpdates' // Ensure the side panel is visible for testing
  | '--enable-unsafe-extension-debugging' // Allow CDP-based extension management (Chrome 126+)
  | '--silent-debugger-extension-api' // Suppress the "X is debugging this browser" infobar

/**
 * Options for the browser plugin.
 */
export interface PluginOptions {
  /**
   * Do not open the browser automatically after launch.
   * @default false
   */
  noOpen?: boolean

  /**
   * Additional browser flags to pass to the browser process.
   * Example: ['--disable-extensions', '--disable-gpu']
   */
  browserFlags?: string[]

  /**
   * Array of browser flags to exclude from the default set.
   * Example: ['--hide-scrollbars', '--mute-audio']
   */
  excludeBrowserFlags?: Array<DefaultBrowserFlags | string>

  /**
   * Path to the browser profile directory, or false for a temporary profile.
   * Example: 'dist/extension' or false
   */
  profile?: string | false

  /**
   * Use a persistent managed profile for development.
   * Defaults to false (ephemeral temp profiles are used).
   */
  persistProfile?: boolean

  /**
   * Browser preferences object.
   * Example: { extensions: ['dist/extension', 'dist/extension2'] }
   */
  preferences?: Record<string, unknown>

  /**
   * Firefox only: persist changes made to the profile directory.
   */
  keepProfileChanges?: boolean

  /**
   * Firefox only: copy an existing profile before launching.
   */
  copyFromProfile?: string

  /**
   * URL to open when the browser starts.
   * Example: 'http://localhost:3000'
   */
  startingUrl?: string

  /**
   * Enable the browser console.
   * @default false
   */
  browserConsole?: boolean

  /**
   * Open DevTools automatically.
   * @default false
   */
  devtools?: boolean

  /**
   * Path to the Chromium binary.
   * Example: '/path/to/chromium'
   */
  chromiumBinary?: string

  /**
   * Path to the Gecko (Firefox) binary.
   * Example: '/path/to/gecko'
   */
  geckoBinary?: string
}

/**
 * Main interface for the browser plugin.
 */
export interface PluginInterface extends PluginOptions {
  /**
   * Browser type to launch.
   * @default 'chrome'
   * @see DevOptions['browser']
   * Example: 'chrome' | 'edge' | 'firefox'
   */
  browser: BrowserType

  /**
   * Path(s) to the extension(s) to load.
   * Example: 'dist/extension' or ['dist/extension', 'dist/extension2']
   */
  extension: string | string[]

  /**
   * Port to use for the extension or debugging.
   * Example: 12345 or '12345'
   */
  port?: string | number

  /**
   * Internal auto-generated instance ID (not user-configurable).
   * Example: '1234567890'
   */
  instanceId?: string

  /**
   * Path to source files for inspection.
   * Example: 'dist/extension'
   */
  source?: string

  /**
   * Whether to watch the source directory for changes.
   * @default false
   */
  watchSource?: boolean

  /**
   * Output format for source inspection HTML.
   */
  sourceFormat?: 'pretty' | 'json' | 'ndjson'

  /**
   * Output a compact summary instead of full HTML.
   * @default false
   */
  sourceSummary?: boolean

  /**
   * Output page metadata (readyState, viewport, frames).
   */
  sourceMeta?: boolean

  /**
   * CSS selectors to probe for debugging.
   */
  sourceProbe?: string[]

  /**
   * Output a compact extension root tree.
   */
  sourceTree?: 'off' | 'root-only'

  /**
   * Output console summary (best-effort).
   */
  sourceConsole?: boolean

  /**
   * Output DOM snapshots and diffs.
   */
  sourceDom?: boolean

  /**
   * Maximum HTML bytes to print (0 disables truncation).
   */
  sourceMaxBytes?: number

  /**
   * Redaction mode for HTML output.
   */
  sourceRedact?: 'off' | 'safe' | 'strict'

  /**
   * Control Shadow DOM inclusion when extracting HTML.
   */
  sourceIncludeShadow?: 'off' | 'open-only' | 'all'

  /**
   * Include diff metadata on watch updates.
   */
  sourceDiff?: boolean

  /**
   * Log level for unified logger (Chromium CDP logging).
   * One of: 'off', 'error', 'warn', 'info', 'debug', 'trace', 'all'
   */
  logLevel?: 'off' | 'error' | 'warn' | 'info' | 'debug' | 'trace' | 'all'

  /**
   * Log contexts to enable.
   * Example: ['background', 'content', 'page']
   */
  logContexts?: Array<
    | 'background'
    | 'content'
    | 'page'
    | 'sidebar'
    | 'popup'
    | 'options'
    | 'devtools'
  >

  /**
   * Log output format.
   * One of: 'pretty', 'json'
   */
  logFormat?: 'pretty' | 'json' | 'ndjson'

  /**
   * Include timestamps in logs.
   * @default true
   */
  logTimestamps?: boolean

  /**
   * Enable colored log output.
   * @default false
   */
  logColor?: boolean

  /**
   * URL to send logs to.
   * Example: 'http://localhost:3000'
   */
  logUrl?: string

  /**
   * Tab ID or index for logging context.
   */
  logTab?: number | string

  /**
   * Perform a dry run without launching the browser.
   * @default false
   */
  dryRun?: boolean
}

/**
 * Runtime state shared with post-launch setup helpers (CDP/RDP).
 * Extends the main PluginInterface with transient fields used during runtime.
 */
export interface PluginRuntime extends PluginInterface {
  // Intentionally empty for shared runtime; per-run runtime fields live in
  // run-chromium/chromium-types.ts and run-firefox/firefox-types.ts
}

export type LogLevel =
  | 'off'
  | 'error'
  | 'warn'
  | 'info'
  | 'debug'
  | 'trace'
  | 'all'

export type LogContext =
  | 'background'
  | 'content'
  | 'page'
  | 'sidebar'
  | 'popup'
  | 'options'
  | 'devtools'

export type LogFormat = 'pretty' | 'json' | 'ndjson'

/**
 * Unified controller interface used by post-launch logging flows.
 * Implemented by both CDP and RDP controllers.
 */
export interface Controller {
  enableUnifiedLogging: (opts: {
    level?: string
    contexts?: string[] | undefined
    urlFilter?: string | undefined
    tabFilter?: number | string | undefined
    format?: 'pretty' | 'json' | 'ndjson'
    timestamps?: boolean
    color?: boolean
  }) => Promise<void>
  /**
   * Optional event subscription hook for protocol events (CDP/RDP).
   * Present in Chromium CDP flows; Firefox may omit it.
   */
  onProtocolEvent?: (
    cb: (evt: {method?: string; params?: unknown}) => void
  ) => void
}

trace.content:
Now I have a complete map of every `any` and unsafe cast. Here's my plan:

**Core problem**: CDP target lists, protocol events, execution contexts, DOM tree walks, and compiler hooks all flow through `any`. The same pattern repeats — targets read off `getTargets()` as `Record<string, unknown>[]`, then fields are pulled via `(t as any)?.url`.

**Solution**: Add proper interfaces for these recurring shapes in `chromium-types.ts`, then replace every `any` with the correct type. This covers:

1. **`CdpTargetInfo`** — the shape returned by `Target.getTargets` (url, type, targetId, title)
2. **`CdpDomNode`** — recursive DOM node from `DOM.getDocument` 
3. **`CdpExecutionContext`** — execution context shape from `Runtime.executionContextCreated`
4. **`CdpProtocolMessage`** — the shape of a protocol event with method, params, sessionId
5. **Rspack compiler/stats interfaces** — for `apply(compiler)` and `stats` callback
6. **`PageMetaSnapshot`** / `ExtensionRootTree`** — return types for methods currently returning `any`
7. **Console counts** — proper key typing instead of indexing via `as any`

Let me start adding the types and fixing every file.

trace.content:
Done. Here's what changed across **10 files**:

### New types added to `chromium-types.ts`

| Type | Replaces | Used for |
|---|---|---|
| `CdpTargetInfo` | `Record<string, unknown>` with `(t as any)?.url` | Target lists from `Target.getTargets` |
| `CdpDomNode` | `any` recursive node | DOM tree walk in `getClosedShadowRoots` |
| `CdpExecutionContext` | `any` / untyped context objects | `Runtime.executionContextCreated` payloads |
| `CdpProtocolMessage` | `Record<string, unknown>` cast via `as any` | Protocol event handler params |
| `RspackCompiler` | `compiler: any` | Plugin `apply()` methods |
| `RspackStats` | `stats: any` | `hooks.done.tapPromise` callbacks |
| `ExtensionRootTree` / `ExtensionRootTreeNode` | `tree: any` return type | Extension root tree evaluation |
| `PageMetaSnapshot` | `return snapshot as any` | Page metadata evaluation |
| `ConsoleCountKey` | `(this.consoleCounts as any)[normalized]` | Console event counting |

### What was removed

- **Every `as any` cast** in the chromium runner code (40+ occurrences)
- **Every `any` parameter/return type** (`compiler: any`, `stats: any`, `Promise<any[]>`, `Map<number, any>`, `arg: any`)
- **Every `(globalThis as any)`** replaced with properly augmented `typeof globalThis & {...}`

### Verification

- `tsc --noEmit`: **0 errors** (clean compile)
- `vitest run`: **421 tests pass**, 6 fail (same 6 pre-existing failures on the baseline commit — unrelated banner/message formatting tests)
- **Zero runtime behavior changes** — every fix narrows the type without altering control flow
